name: elastic

globalOptions.name: use-context
globalOptions.summary: override the active context from the config file

globalOptions.name: command-profile
globalOptions.summary: restrict available commands to a deployment profile (serverless, stack, default)

globalOptions.name: output-fields
globalOptions.summary: comma-separated list of fields to include in output (dot-notation supported)

globalOptions.name: output-template
globalOptions.summary: Mustache-like template for custom text output (e.g. "{{id}}: {{name}}")

globalOptions.name: dry-run
globalOptions.summary: validate all inputs and exit without performing any action

variables.name: ELASTIC_CLI_CONFIG_FILE
variables.description: Override the config file path (precedence: --config-file > this > home directory discovery)

variables.name: ELASTIC_NO_BANNER
variables.description: Set to 1 to suppress the startup logo

variables.name: ELASTIC_CLOUD_ADMIN_API
variables.description: Override the Elastic Cloud admin API base URL

configFiles.description: JSON form of the config file
Extensionless form of the config file

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: The name of the data stream, index, or index alias to perform bulk actions on.

parameters.name: include-source-on-error
parameters.summary: True or false if to include the document source in the error message in case of parsing errors.

parameters.name: list-executed-pipelines
parameters.summary: If `true`, the response will include the ingest pipelines that were run for each index or create.

parameters.name: pipeline
parameters.summary: The pipeline identifier to use to preprocess incoming documents. If the index has a default ingest pipeline specified, setting the value to `_none` turns off the default ingest pipeline for this request. If a final pipeline is configured, it will always run regardless of the value of this parameter.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.summary: If `true`, Elasticsearch refreshes the affected shards to make this operation visible to search. If `wait_for`, wait for a refresh to make this operation visible to search. If `false`, do nothing with refreshes. Valid values: `true`, `false`, `wait_for`.

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: A custom value that is used to route operations to a specific shard.

parameters.name: source
parameters.summary: Indicates whether to return the `_source` field (`true` or `false`) or contains a list of fields to return.

parameters.name: source-excludes
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of source fields to exclude from the response. You can also use this parameter to exclude fields from the subset specified in `_source_includes` query parameter. If the `_source` parameter is `false`, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: source-includes
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of source fields to include in the response. If this parameter is specified, only these source fields are returned. You can exclude fields from this subset using the `_source_excludes` query parameter. If the `_source` parameter is `false`, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: The period each action waits for the following operations: automatic index creation, dynamic mapping updates, and waiting for active shards. The default is `1m` (one minute), which guarantees Elasticsearch waits for at least the timeout before failing. The actual wait time could be longer, particularly when multiple waits occur.

parameters.name: wait-for-active-shards
parameters.summary: The number of shard copies that must be active before proceeding with the operation. Set to `all` or any positive integer up to the total number of shards in the index (`number_of_replicas+1`). The default is `1`, which waits for each primary shard to be active.

parameters.name: require-alias
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request's actions must target an index alias.

parameters.name: require-data-stream
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request's actions must target a data stream (existing or to be created).

commands.name: create

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: A unique identifier for the document. To automatically generate a document ID, use the `POST /<target>/_doc/` request format.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: The name of the data stream or index to target. If the target doesn't exist and matches the name or wildcard (`*`) pattern of an index template with a `data_stream` definition, this request creates the data stream. If the target doesn't exist and doesn’t match a data stream template, this request creates the index.

parameters.name: include-source-on-error
parameters.summary: True or false if to include the document source in the error message in case of parsing errors.

parameters.name: pipeline
parameters.summary: The ID of the pipeline to use to preprocess incoming documents. If the index has a default ingest pipeline specified, setting the value to `_none` turns off the default ingest pipeline for this request. If a final pipeline is configured, it will always run regardless of the value of this parameter.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.summary: If `true`, Elasticsearch refreshes the affected shards to make this operation visible to search. If `wait_for`, it waits for a refresh to make this operation visible to search. If `false`, it does nothing with refreshes.

parameters.name: require-alias
parameters.summary: If `true`, the destination must be an index alias.

parameters.name: require-data-stream
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request's actions must target a data stream (existing or to be created).

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: A custom value that is used to route operations to a specific shard.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: The period the request waits for the following operations: automatic index creation, dynamic mapping updates, waiting for active shards. Elasticsearch waits for at least the specified timeout period before failing. The actual wait time could be longer, particularly when multiple waits occur. This parameter is useful for situations where the primary shard assigned to perform the operation might not be available when the operation runs. Some reasons for this might be that the primary shard is currently recovering from a gateway or undergoing relocation. By default, the operation will wait on the primary shard to become available for at least 1 minute before failing and responding with an error. The actual wait time could be longer, particularly when multiple waits occur.

parameters.name: version
parameters.summary: The explicit version number for concurrency control. It must be a non-negative long number.

parameters.name: version-type
parameters.summary: The version type.

parameters.name: wait-for-active-shards
parameters.summary: The number of shard copies that must be active before proceeding with the operation. You can set it to `all` or any positive integer up to the total number of shards in the index (`number_of_replicas+1`). The default value of `1` means it waits for each primary shard to be active.

commands.summary: Create a new document in the index.

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: A unique identifier for the document.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: The name of the target index.

parameters.name: if-primary-term
parameters.summary: Only perform the operation if the document has this primary term.

parameters.name: if-seq-no
parameters.summary: Only perform the operation if the document has this sequence number.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.summary: If `true`, Elasticsearch refreshes the affected shards to make this operation visible to search. If `wait_for`, it waits for a refresh to make this operation visible to search. If `false`, it does nothing with refreshes.

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: A custom value used to route operations to a specific shard.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: The period to wait for active shards. This parameter is useful for situations where the primary shard assigned to perform the delete operation might not be available when the delete operation runs. Some reasons for this might be that the primary shard is currently recovering from a store or undergoing relocation. By default, the delete operation will wait on the primary shard to become available for up to 1 minute before failing and responding with an error.

parameters.name: version
parameters.summary: An explicit version number for concurrency control. It must match the current version of the document for the request to succeed.

parameters.name: version-type
parameters.summary: The version type.

parameters.name: wait-for-active-shards
parameters.summary: The minimum number of shard copies that must be active before proceeding with the operation. You can set it to `all` or any positive integer up to the total number of shards in the index (`number_of_replicas+1`). The default value of `1` means it waits for each primary shard to be active.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of data streams, indices, and aliases to search. It supports wildcards (`*`). To search all data streams or indices, omit this parameter or use `*` or `_all`.

parameters.name: allow-no-indices
parameters.summary: A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression. If `false`, the request returns an error (1) if any wildcard expression (including `_all` and `*`) resolves to zero matching indices or (2) if the complete set of resolved indices, aliases or data streams is empty after all expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.name: analyzer
parameters.summary: Analyzer to use for the query string. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: analyze-wildcard
parameters.summary: If `true`, wildcard and prefix queries are analyzed. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: conflicts
parameters.summary: What to do if delete by query hits version conflicts: `abort` or `proceed`.

parameters.name: default-operator
parameters.summary: The default operator for query string query: `and` or `or`. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: df
parameters.summary: The field to use as default where no field prefix is given in the query string. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: expand-wildcards
parameters.summary: The type of index that wildcard patterns can match. If the request can target data streams, this argument determines whether wildcard expressions match hidden data streams. It supports comma-separated values, such as `open,hidden`.

parameters.name: from
parameters.summary: Skips the specified number of documents.

parameters.name: ignore-unavailable
parameters.summary: If `false`, the request returns an error if it targets a concrete (non-wildcarded) index, alias, or data stream that is missing, closed, or otherwise unavailable. If `true`, unavailable concrete targets are silently ignored.

parameters.name: lenient
parameters.summary: If `true`, format-based query failures (such as providing text to a numeric field) in the query string will be ignored. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: preference
parameters.summary: The node or shard the operation should be performed on. It is random by default.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.summary: If `true`, Elasticsearch refreshes all shards involved in the delete by query after the request completes. This is different than the delete API's `refresh` parameter, which causes just the shard that received the delete request to be refreshed. Unlike the delete API, it does not support `wait_for`.

parameters.name: request-cache
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request cache is used for this request. Defaults to the index-level setting.

parameters.name: requests-per-second
parameters.summary: The throttle for this request in sub-requests per second.

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: A custom value used to route operations to a specific shard.

parameters.name: q
parameters.summary: A query in the Lucene query string syntax.

parameters.name: scroll
parameters.summary: The period to retain the search context for scrolling.

parameters.name: scroll-size
parameters.summary: The size of the scroll request that powers the operation.

parameters.name: search-timeout
parameters.summary: The explicit timeout for each search request. It defaults to no timeout.

parameters.name: search-type
parameters.summary: The type of the search operation. Available options include `query_then_fetch` and `dfs_query_then_fetch`.

parameters.name: slices
parameters.summary: The number of slices this task should be divided into.

parameters.name: stats
parameters.summary: The specific `tag` of the request for logging and statistical purposes.

parameters.name: terminate-after
parameters.summary: The maximum number of documents to collect for each shard. If a query reaches this limit, Elasticsearch terminates the query early. Elasticsearch collects documents before sorting. Use with caution. Elasticsearch applies this parameter to each shard handling the request. When possible, let Elasticsearch perform early termination automatically. Avoid specifying this parameter for requests that target data streams with backing indices across multiple data tiers.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: The period each deletion request waits for active shards.

parameters.name: version
parameters.summary: If `true`, returns the document version as part of a hit.

parameters.name: wait-for-active-shards
parameters.summary: The number of shard copies that must be active before proceeding with the operation. Set to `all` or any positive integer up to the total number of shards in the index (`number_of_replicas+1`). The `timeout` value controls how long each write request waits for unavailable shards to become available.

parameters.name: wait-for-completion
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request blocks until the operation is complete. If `false`, Elasticsearch performs some preflight checks, launches the request, and returns a task you can use to cancel or get the status of the task. Elasticsearch creates a record of this task as a document at `.tasks/task/{taskId}`. When you are done with a task, you should delete the task document so Elasticsearch can reclaim the space.

parameters.name: max-docs
parameters.summary: The maximum number of documents to delete.

parameters.name: query
parameters.summary: The documents to delete specified with Query DSL.

parameters.name: slice
parameters.summary: Slice the request manually using the provided slice ID and total number of slices.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.summary: A sort object that specifies the order of deleted documents.

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: A unique document identifier.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of data streams, indices, and aliases. It supports wildcards (`*`).

parameters.name: preference
parameters.summary: The node or shard the operation should be performed on. By default, the operation is randomized between the shard replicas. If it is set to `_local`, the operation will prefer to be run on a local allocated shard when possible. If it is set to a custom value, the value is used to guarantee that the same shards will be used for the same custom value. This can help with "jumping values" when hitting different shards in different refresh states. A sample value can be something like the web session ID or the user name.

parameters.name: realtime
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request is real-time as opposed to near-real-time.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request refreshes the relevant shards before retrieving the document. Setting it to `true` should be done after careful thought and verification that this does not cause a heavy load on the system (and slow down indexing).

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: A custom value used to route operations to a specific shard.

parameters.name: source
parameters.summary: Indicates whether to return the `_source` field (`true` or `false`) or lists the fields to return.

parameters.name: source-excludes
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of source fields to exclude from the response. You can also use this parameter to exclude fields from the subset specified in `_source_includes` query parameter. If the `_source` parameter is `false`, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: source-includes
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of source fields to include in the response. If this parameter is specified, only these source fields are returned. You can exclude fields from this subset using the `_source_excludes` query parameter. If the `_source` parameter is `false`, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: stored-fields
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of stored fields to return as part of a hit. If no fields are specified, no stored fields are included in the response. If this field is specified, the `_source` parameter defaults to `false`.

parameters.summary: Explicit version number for concurrency control. The specified version must match the current version of the document for the request to succeed.

commands.name: index

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: A unique identifier for the document. To automatically generate a document ID, use the `POST /<target>/_doc/` request format and omit this parameter.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: The name of the data stream or index to target. If the target doesn't exist and matches the name or wildcard (`*`) pattern of an index template with a `data_stream` definition, this request creates the data stream. If the target doesn't exist and doesn't match a data stream template, this request creates the index. You can check for existing targets with the resolve index API.

parameters.name: if-primary-term
parameters.summary: Only perform the operation if the document has this primary term.

parameters.name: if-seq-no
parameters.summary: Only perform the operation if the document has this sequence number.

parameters.name: include-source-on-error
parameters.summary: True or false if to include the document source in the error message in case of parsing errors.

parameters.name: op-type
parameters.summary: Set to `create` to only index the document if it does not already exist (put if absent). If a document with the specified `_id` already exists, the indexing operation will fail. The behavior is the same as using the `<index>/_create` endpoint. If a document ID is specified, this paramater defaults to `index`. Otherwise, it defaults to `create`. If the request targets a data stream, an `op_type` of `create` is required.

parameters.name: pipeline
parameters.summary: The ID of the pipeline to use to preprocess incoming documents. If the index has a default ingest pipeline specified, then setting the value to `_none` disables the default ingest pipeline for this request. If a final pipeline is configured it will always run, regardless of the value of this parameter.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.summary: If `true`, Elasticsearch refreshes the affected shards to make this operation visible to search. If `wait_for`, it waits for a refresh to make this operation visible to search. If `false`, it does nothing with refreshes.

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: A custom value that is used to route operations to a specific shard.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: The period the request waits for the following operations: automatic index creation, dynamic mapping updates, waiting for active shards. This parameter is useful for situations where the primary shard assigned to perform the operation might not be available when the operation runs. Some reasons for this might be that the primary shard is currently recovering from a gateway or undergoing relocation. By default, the operation will wait on the primary shard to become available for at least 1 minute before failing and responding with an error. The actual wait time could be longer, particularly when multiple waits occur.

parameters.name: version
parameters.summary: An explicit version number for concurrency control. It must be a non-negative long number.

parameters.name: version-type
parameters.summary: The version type.

parameters.name: wait-for-active-shards
parameters.summary: The number of shard copies that must be active before proceeding with the operation. You can set it to `all` or any positive integer up to the total number of shards in the index (`number_of_replicas+1`). The default value of `1` means it waits for each primary shard to be active.

parameters.name: require-alias
parameters.summary: If `true`, the destination must be an index alias.

parameters.name: require-data-stream
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request's actions must target a data stream (existing or to be created).

commands.summary: Create or update a document in an index.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: Name of the index to retrieve documents from when `ids` are specified, or when a document in the `docs` array does not specify an index.

parameters.name: preference
parameters.summary: Specifies the node or shard the operation should be performed on. Random by default.

parameters.name: realtime
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request is real-time as opposed to near-real-time.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request refreshes relevant shards before retrieving documents.

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: Custom value used to route operations to a specific shard.

parameters.name: source
parameters.summary: True or false to return the `_source` field or not, or a list of fields to return.

parameters.name: source-excludes
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of source fields to exclude from the response. You can also use this parameter to exclude fields from the subset specified in `_source_includes` query parameter.

parameters.name: source-includes
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of source fields to include in the response. If this parameter is specified, only these source fields are returned. You can exclude fields from this subset using the `_source_excludes` query parameter. If the `_source` parameter is `false`, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: stored-fields
parameters.summary: If `true`, retrieves the document fields stored in the index rather than the document `_source`.

parameters.name: docs
parameters.summary: The documents you want to retrieve. Required if no index is specified in the request URI.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.summary: The IDs of the documents you want to retrieve. Allowed when the index is specified in the request URI.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request refreshes affected shards to make this operation visible to search.

parameters.name: requests-per-second
parameters.summary: The throttle for this request in sub-requests per second. By default, there is no throttle.

parameters.name: scroll
parameters.summary: The period of time that a consistent view of the index should be maintained for scrolled search.

parameters.name: slices
parameters.summary: The number of slices this task should be divided into. It defaults to one slice, which means the task isn't sliced into subtasks. Reindex supports sliced scroll to parallelize the reindexing process. This parallelization can improve efficiency and provide a convenient way to break the request down into smaller parts. NOTE: Reindexing from remote clusters does not support manual or automatic slicing. If set to `auto`, Elasticsearch chooses the number of slices to use. This setting will use one slice per shard, up to a certain limit. If there are multiple sources, it will choose the number of slices based on the index or backing index with the smallest number of shards.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: The period each indexing waits for automatic index creation, dynamic mapping updates, and waiting for active shards. By default, Elasticsearch waits for at least one minute before failing. The actual wait time could be longer, particularly when multiple waits occur.

parameters.name: wait-for-active-shards
parameters.summary: The number of shard copies that must be active before proceeding with the operation. Set it to `all` or any positive integer up to the total number of shards in the index (`number_of_replicas+1`). The default value is one, which means it waits for each primary shard to be active.

parameters.name: wait-for-completion
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request blocks until the operation is complete.

parameters.name: require-alias
parameters.summary: If `true`, the destination must be an index alias.

parameters.name: conflicts
parameters.summary: Indicates whether to continue reindexing even when there are conflicts.

parameters.name: dest
parameters.summary: The destination you are copying to.

parameters.name: max-docs
parameters.summary: The maximum number of documents to reindex. By default, all documents are reindexed. If it is a value less then or equal to `scroll_size`, a scroll will not be used to retrieve the results for the operation. If `conflicts` is set to `proceed`, the reindex operation could attempt to reindex more documents from the source than `max_docs` until it has successfully indexed `max_docs` documents into the target or it has gone through every document in the source query.

parameters.name: script
parameters.summary: The script to run to update the document source or metadata when reindexing.

parameters.name: source
parameters.summary: The source you are copying from.

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: A unique identifier for the document to be updated.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: The name of the target index. By default, the index is created automatically if it doesn't exist.

parameters.name: if-primary-term
parameters.summary: Only perform the operation if the document has this primary term.

parameters.name: if-seq-no
parameters.summary: Only perform the operation if the document has this sequence number.

parameters.name: include-source-on-error
parameters.summary: True or false if to include the document source in the error message in case of parsing errors.

parameters.name: lang
parameters.summary: The script language.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.summary: If 'true', Elasticsearch refreshes the affected shards to make this operation visible to search. If 'wait_for', it waits for a refresh to make this operation visible to search. If 'false', it does nothing with refreshes.

parameters.name: require-alias
parameters.summary: If `true`, the destination must be an index alias.

parameters.name: retry-on-conflict
parameters.summary: The number of times the operation should be retried when a conflict occurs.

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: A custom value used to route operations to a specific shard.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: The period to wait for the following operations: dynamic mapping updates and waiting for active shards. Elasticsearch waits for at least the timeout period before failing. The actual wait time could be longer, particularly when multiple waits occur.

parameters.name: wait-for-active-shards
parameters.summary: The number of copies of each shard that must be active before proceeding with the operation. Set to 'all' or any positive integer up to the total number of shards in the index (`number_of_replicas`+1). The default value of `1` means it waits for each primary shard to be active.

parameters.name: source-excludes
parameters.summary: The source fields you want to exclude.

parameters.name: source-includes
parameters.summary: The source fields you want to retrieve.

parameters.name: detect-noop
parameters.summary: If `true`, the `result` in the response is set to `noop` (no operation) when there are no changes to the document.

parameters.name: doc
parameters.summary: A partial update to an existing document. If both `doc` and `script` are specified, `doc` is ignored.

parameters.name: doc-as-upsert
parameters.summary: If `true`, use the contents of 'doc' as the value of 'upsert'. NOTE: Using ingest pipelines with `doc_as_upsert` is not supported.

parameters.name: script
parameters.summary: The script to run to update the document.

parameters.name: scripted-upsert
parameters.summary: If `true`, run the script whether or not the document exists.

parameters.name: source
parameters.summary: If `false`, turn off source retrieval. You can also specify a comma-separated list of the fields you want to retrieve.

parameters.name: upsert
parameters.summary: If the document does not already exist, the contents of 'upsert' are inserted as a new document. If the document exists, the 'script' is run.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of data streams, indices, and aliases to search. It supports wildcards (`*`). To search all data streams or indices, omit this parameter or use `*` or `_all`.

parameters.name: allow-no-indices
parameters.summary: A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression. If `false`, the request returns an error (1) if any wildcard expression (including `_all` and `*`) resolves to zero matching indices or (2) if the complete set of resolved indices, aliases or data streams is empty after all expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.name: analyzer
parameters.summary: The analyzer to use for the query string. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: analyze-wildcard
parameters.summary: If `true`, wildcard and prefix queries are analyzed. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: default-operator
parameters.summary: The default operator for query string query: `and` or `or`. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: df
parameters.summary: The field to use as default where no field prefix is given in the query string. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: expand-wildcards
parameters.summary: The type of index that wildcard patterns can match. If the request can target data streams, this argument determines whether wildcard expressions match hidden data streams. It supports comma-separated values, such as `open,hidden`.

parameters.name: from
parameters.summary: Skips the specified number of documents.

parameters.name: ignore-unavailable
parameters.summary: If `false`, the request returns an error if it targets a concrete (non-wildcarded) index, alias, or data stream that is missing, closed, or otherwise unavailable. If `true`, unavailable concrete targets are silently ignored.

parameters.name: lenient
parameters.summary: If `true`, format-based query failures (such as providing text to a numeric field) in the query string will be ignored. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: pipeline
parameters.summary: The ID of the pipeline to use to preprocess incoming documents. If the index has a default ingest pipeline specified, then setting the value to `_none` disables the default ingest pipeline for this request. If a final pipeline is configured it will always run, regardless of the value of this parameter.

parameters.name: preference
parameters.summary: The node or shard the operation should be performed on. It is random by default.

parameters.name: q
parameters.summary: A query in the Lucene query string syntax.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.summary: If `true`, Elasticsearch refreshes affected shards to make the operation visible to search after the request completes. This is different than the update API's `refresh` parameter, which causes just the shard that received the request to be refreshed.

parameters.name: request-cache
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request cache is used for this request. It defaults to the index-level setting.

parameters.name: requests-per-second
parameters.summary: The throttle for this request in sub-requests per second.

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: A custom value used to route operations to a specific shard.

parameters.name: scroll
parameters.summary: The period to retain the search context for scrolling.

parameters.name: scroll-size
parameters.summary: The size of the scroll request that powers the operation.

parameters.name: search-timeout
parameters.summary: An explicit timeout for each search request. By default, there is no timeout.

parameters.name: search-type
parameters.summary: The type of the search operation. Available options include `query_then_fetch` and `dfs_query_then_fetch`.

parameters.name: slices
parameters.summary: The number of slices this task should be divided into.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of <field>:<direction> pairs.

parameters.name: stats
parameters.summary: The specific `tag` of the request for logging and statistical purposes.

parameters.name: terminate-after
parameters.summary: The maximum number of documents to collect for each shard. If a query reaches this limit, Elasticsearch terminates the query early. Elasticsearch collects documents before sorting. IMPORTANT: Use with caution. Elasticsearch applies this parameter to each shard handling the request. When possible, let Elasticsearch perform early termination automatically. Avoid specifying this parameter for requests that target data streams with backing indices across multiple data tiers.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: The period each update request waits for the following operations: dynamic mapping updates, waiting for active shards. By default, it is one minute. This guarantees Elasticsearch waits for at least the timeout before failing. The actual wait time could be longer, particularly when multiple waits occur.

parameters.name: version
parameters.summary: If `true`, returns the document version as part of a hit.

parameters.name: version-type
parameters.summary: Should the document increment the version number (internal) on hit or not (reindex)

parameters.name: wait-for-active-shards
parameters.summary: The number of shard copies that must be active before proceeding with the operation. Set to `all` or any positive integer up to the total number of shards in the index (`number_of_replicas+1`). The `timeout` parameter controls how long each write request waits for unavailable shards to become available. Both work exactly the way they work in the bulk API.

parameters.name: wait-for-completion
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request blocks until the operation is complete. If `false`, Elasticsearch performs some preflight checks, launches the request, and returns a task ID that you can use to cancel or get the status of the task. Elasticsearch creates a record of this task as a document at `.tasks/task/{taskId}`.

parameters.name: max-docs
parameters.summary: The maximum number of documents to update.

parameters.name: query
parameters.summary: The documents to update using the Query DSL.

parameters.name: script
parameters.summary: The script to run to update the document source or metadata when updating.

parameters.name: slice
parameters.summary: Slice the request manually using the provided slice ID and total number of slices.

parameters.name: conflicts
parameters.summary: The preferred behavior when update by query hits version conflicts: `abort` or `proceed`.

parameters.name: scroll-id
parameters.summary: The scroll IDs to clear. To clear all scroll IDs, use `_all`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: The ID of the point-in-time.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: Comma-separated list of data streams, indices, and index aliases to search.

parameters.name: allow-no-indices
parameters.summary: A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression. If `false`, the request returns an error (1) if any wildcard expression (including `_all` and `*`) resolves to zero matching indices or (2) if the complete set of resolved indices, aliases or data streams is empty after all expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.name: ccs-minimize-roundtrips
parameters.summary: If true, network roundtrips between the coordinating node and remote clusters are minimized for cross-cluster search requests.

parameters.name: expand-wildcards
parameters.summary: Type of index that wildcard expressions can match. If the request can target data streams, this argument determines whether wildcard expressions match hidden data streams.

parameters.name: ignore-throttled
parameters.summary: If true, concrete, expanded or aliased indices are ignored when frozen.

parameters.name: ignore-unavailable
parameters.summary: If `false`, the request returns an error if it targets a concrete (non-wildcarded) index, alias, or data stream that is missing, closed, or otherwise unavailable. If `true`, unavailable concrete targets are silently ignored.

parameters.name: include-named-queries-score
parameters.summary: Indicates whether hit.matched_queries should be rendered as a map that includes the name of the matched query associated with its score (true) or as an array containing the name of the matched queries (false) This functionality reruns each named query on every hit in a search response. Typically, this adds a small overhead to a request. However, using computationally expensive named queries on a large number of hits may add significant overhead.

parameters.name: max-concurrent-searches
parameters.summary: Maximum number of concurrent searches the multi search API can execute. Defaults to `max(1, (# of data nodes * min(search thread pool size, 10)))`.

parameters.name: max-concurrent-shard-requests
parameters.summary: Maximum number of concurrent shard requests that each sub-search request executes per node.

parameters.name: pre-filter-shard-size
parameters.summary: Defines a threshold that enforces a pre-filter roundtrip to prefilter search shards based on query rewriting if the number of shards the search request expands to exceeds the threshold. This filter roundtrip can limit the number of shards significantly if for instance a shard can not match any documents based on its rewrite method i.e., if date filters are mandatory to match but the shard bounds and the query are disjoint.

parameters.name: rest-total-hits-as-int
parameters.summary: If true, hits.total are returned as an integer in the response. Defaults to false, which returns an object.

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: Custom routing value used to route search operations to a specific shard.

parameters.name: search-type
parameters.summary: Indicates whether global term and document frequencies should be used when scoring returned documents.

parameters.name: typed-keys
parameters.summary: Specifies whether aggregation and suggester names should be prefixed by their respective types in the response.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of data streams, indices, and aliases to search. It supports wildcards (`*`). To search all data streams and indices, omit this parameter or use `*`.

parameters.name: ccs-minimize-roundtrips
parameters.summary: If `true`, network round-trips are minimized for cross-cluster search requests.

parameters.name: max-concurrent-searches
parameters.summary: The maximum number of concurrent searches the API can run.

parameters.name: search-type
parameters.summary: The type of the search operation.

parameters.name: rest-total-hits-as-int
parameters.summary: If `true`, the response returns `hits.total` as an integer. If `false`, it returns `hits.total` as an object.

parameters.name: typed-keys
parameters.summary: If `true`, the response prefixes aggregation and suggester names with their respective types.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of index names to open point in time; use `_all` or empty string to perform the operation on all indices

parameters.name: keep-alive
parameters.summary: Extend the length of time that the point in time persists.

parameters.name: ignore-unavailable
parameters.summary: If `false`, the request returns an error if it targets a concrete (non-wildcarded) index, alias, or data stream that is missing, closed, or otherwise unavailable. If `true`, unavailable concrete targets are silently ignored.

parameters.name: preference
parameters.summary: The node or shard the operation should be performed on. By default, it is random.

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: A custom value that is used to route operations to a specific shard.

parameters.name: expand-wildcards
parameters.summary: The type of index that wildcard patterns can match. If the request can target data streams, this argument determines whether wildcard expressions match hidden data streams. It supports comma-separated values, such as `open,hidden`.

parameters.name: allow-partial-search-results
parameters.summary: Indicates whether the point in time tolerates unavailable shards or shard failures when initially creating the PIT. If `false`, creating a point in time request when a shard is missing or unavailable will throw an exception. If `true`, the point in time will contain all the shards that are available at the time of the request.

parameters.name: max-concurrent-shard-requests
parameters.summary: Maximum number of concurrent shard requests that each sub-search request executes per node.

parameters.name: index-filter
parameters.summary: Filter indices if the provided query rewrites to `match_none` on every shard.

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: The ID of the search template to render. If no `source` is specified, this or the `id` request body parameter is required.

parameters.name: params
parameters.summary: Key-value pairs used to replace Mustache variables in the template. The key is the variable name. The value is the variable value.

parameters.name: source
parameters.summary: An inline search template. It supports the same parameters as the search API's request body. These parameters also support Mustache variables. If no `id` or `<templated-id>` is specified, this parameter is required.

parameters.name: rest-total-hits-as-int
parameters.summary: If true, the API response’s hit.total property is returned as an integer. If false, the API response’s hit.total property is returned as an object.

parameters.name: scroll
parameters.summary: The period to retain the search context for scrolling.

parameters.name: scroll-id
parameters.summary: The scroll ID of the search.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of data streams, indices, and aliases to search. It supports wildcards (`*`). To search all data streams and indices, omit this parameter or use `*` or `_all`.

parameters.name: allow-no-indices
parameters.summary: A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression. If `false`, the request returns an error (1) if any wildcard expression (including `_all` and `*`) resolves to zero matching indices or (2) if the complete set of resolved indices, aliases or data streams is empty after all expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.name: allow-partial-search-results
parameters.summary: If `true` and there are shard request timeouts or shard failures, the request returns partial results. If `false`, it returns an error with no partial results. To override the default behavior, you can set the `search.default_allow_partial_results` cluster setting to `false`.

parameters.name: analyzer
parameters.summary: The analyzer to use for the query string. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: analyze-wildcard
parameters.summary: If `true`, wildcard and prefix queries are analyzed. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: batched-reduce-size
parameters.summary: The number of shard results that should be reduced at once on the coordinating node. If the potential number of shards in the request can be large, this value should be used as a protection mechanism to reduce the memory overhead per search request.

parameters.name: ccs-minimize-roundtrips
parameters.summary: If `true`, network round-trips between the coordinating node and the remote clusters are minimized when running cross-cluster search (CCS) requests.

parameters.name: default-operator
parameters.summary: The default operator for the query string query: `and` or `or`. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: df
parameters.summary: The field to use as a default when no field prefix is given in the query string. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: expand-wildcards
parameters.summary: The type of index that wildcard patterns can match. If the request can target data streams, this argument determines whether wildcard expressions match hidden data streams. It supports comma-separated values such as `open,hidden`.

parameters.name: ignore-throttled
parameters.summary: If `true`, concrete, expanded or aliased indices will be ignored when frozen.

parameters.name: ignore-unavailable
parameters.summary: If `false`, the request returns an error if it targets a concrete (non-wildcarded) index, alias, or data stream that is missing, closed, or otherwise unavailable. If `true`, unavailable concrete targets are silently ignored.

parameters.name: include-named-queries-score
parameters.summary: If `true`, the response includes the score contribution from any named queries. This functionality reruns each named query on every hit in a search response. Typically, this adds a small overhead to a request. However, using computationally expensive named queries on a large number of hits may add significant overhead.

parameters.name: lenient
parameters.summary: If `true`, format-based query failures (such as providing text to a numeric field) in the query string will be ignored. This parameter can be used only when the `q` query string parameter is specified.

parameters.name: max-concurrent-shard-requests
parameters.summary: The number of concurrent shard requests per node that the search runs concurrently. This value should be used to limit the impact of the search on the cluster in order to limit the number of concurrent shard requests.

parameters.name: preference
parameters.summary: The nodes and shards used for the search. By default, Elasticsearch selects from eligible nodes and shards using adaptive replica selection, accounting for allocation awareness. Valid values are: * `_only_local` to run the search only on shards on the local node. * `_local` to, if possible, run the search on shards on the local node, or if not, select shards using the default method. * `_only_nodes:<node-id>,<node-id>` to run the search on only the specified nodes IDs. If suitable shards exist on more than one selected node, use shards on those nodes using the default method. If none of the specified nodes are available, select shards from any available node using the default method. * `_prefer_nodes:<node-id>,<node-id>` to if possible, run the search on the specified nodes IDs. If not, select shards using the default method. * `_shards:<shard>,<shard>` to run the search only on the specified shards. You can combine this value with other `preference` values. However, the `_shards` value must come first. For example: `_shards:2,3|_local`. * `<custom-string>` (any string that does not start with `_`) to route searches with the same `<custom-string>` to the same shards in the same order.

parameters.name: pre-filter-shard-size
parameters.summary: A threshold that enforces a pre-filter roundtrip to prefilter search shards based on query rewriting if the number of shards the search request expands to exceeds the threshold. This filter roundtrip can limit the number of shards significantly if for instance a shard can not match any documents based on its rewrite method (if date filters are mandatory to match but the shard bounds and the query are disjoint). When unspecified, the pre-filter phase is executed if any of these conditions is met: * The request targets more than 128 shards. * The request targets one or more read-only index. * The primary sort of the query targets an indexed field.

parameters.name: request-cache
parameters.summary: If `true`, the caching of search results is enabled for requests where `size` is `0`. It defaults to index level settings.

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: A custom value that is used to route operations to a specific shard.

parameters.name: scroll
parameters.summary: The period to retain the search context for scrolling. By default, this value cannot exceed `1d` (24 hours). You can change this limit by using the `search.max_keep_alive` cluster-level setting.

parameters.name: search-type
parameters.summary: Indicates how distributed term frequencies are calculated for relevance scoring.

parameters.name: suggest-field
parameters.summary: The field to use for suggestions.

parameters.name: suggest-mode
parameters.summary: The suggest mode. This parameter can be used only when the `suggest_field` and `suggest_text` query string parameters are specified.

parameters.name: suggest-size
parameters.summary: The number of suggestions to return. This parameter can be used only when the `suggest_field` and `suggest_text` query string parameters are specified.

parameters.name: suggest-text
parameters.summary: The source text for which the suggestions should be returned. This parameter can be used only when the `suggest_field` and `suggest_text` query string parameters are specified.

parameters.name: typed-keys
parameters.summary: If `true`, aggregation and suggester names are be prefixed by their respective types in the response.

parameters.name: rest-total-hits-as-int
parameters.summary: Indicates whether `hits.total` should be rendered as an integer or an object in the rest search response.

parameters.name: source-excludes
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of source fields to exclude from the response. You can also use this parameter to exclude fields from the subset specified in `_source_includes` query parameter. If the `_source` parameter is `false`, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: source-exclude-vectors
parameters.summary: Whether vectors should be excluded from _source

parameters.name: source-includes
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of source fields to include in the response. If this parameter is specified, only these source fields are returned. You can exclude fields from this subset using the `_source_excludes` query parameter. If the `_source` parameter is `false`, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: q
parameters.summary: A query in the Lucene query string syntax. Query parameter searches do not support the full Elasticsearch Query DSL but are handy for testing. IMPORTANT: This parameter overrides the query parameter in the request body. If both parameters are specified, documents matching the query request body parameter are not returned.

parameters.name: aggregations
parameters.summary: Defines the aggregations that are run as part of the search request.

parameters.name: aggs
parameters.summary: Defines the aggregations that are run as part of the search request.

parameters.name: collapse
parameters.summary: Collapses search results the values of the specified field.

parameters.name: explain
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request returns detailed information about score computation as part of a hit.

parameters.name: ext
parameters.summary: Configuration of search extensions defined by Elasticsearch plugins.

parameters.name: from
parameters.summary: The starting document offset, which must be non-negative. By default, you cannot page through more than 10,000 hits using the `from` and `size` parameters. To page through more hits, use the `search_after` parameter.

parameters.name: highlight
parameters.summary: Specifies the highlighter to use for retrieving highlighted snippets from one or more fields in your search results.

parameters.name: track-total-hits
parameters.summary: Number of hits matching the query to count accurately. If `true`, the exact number of hits is returned at the cost of some performance. If `false`, the  response does not include the total number of hits matching the query.

parameters.name: indices-boost
parameters.summary: Boost the `_score` of documents from specified indices. The boost value is the factor by which scores are multiplied. A boost value greater than `1.0` increases the score. A boost value between `0` and `1.0` decreases the score.

parameters.name: docvalue-fields
parameters.summary: An array of wildcard (`*`) field patterns. The request returns doc values for field names matching these patterns in the `hits.fields` property of the response.

parameters.name: knn
parameters.summary: The approximate kNN search to run.

parameters.name: min-score
parameters.summary: The minimum `_score` for matching documents. Documents with a lower `_score` are not included in search results and results collected by aggregations.

parameters.name: post-filter
parameters.summary: Use the `post_filter` parameter to filter search results. The search hits are filtered after the aggregations are calculated. A post filter has no impact on the aggregation results.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.summary: Set to `true` to return detailed timing information about the execution of individual components in a search request. NOTE: This is a debugging tool and adds significant overhead to search execution.

parameters.name: query
parameters.summary: The search definition using the Query DSL.

parameters.name: rescore
parameters.summary: Can be used to improve precision by reordering just the top (for example 100 - 500) documents returned by the `query` and `post_filter` phases.

parameters.name: retriever
parameters.summary: A retriever is a specification to describe top documents returned from a search. A retriever replaces other elements of the search API that also return top documents such as `query` and `knn`.

parameters.name: script-fields
parameters.summary: Retrieve a script evaluation (based on different fields) for each hit.

parameters.name: search-after
parameters.summary: Used to retrieve the next page of hits using a set of sort values from the previous page.

parameters.name: size
parameters.summary: The number of hits to return, which must not be negative. By default, you cannot page through more than 10,000 hits using the `from` and `size` parameters. To page through more hits, use the `search_after` property.

parameters.name: slice
parameters.summary: Split a scrolled search into multiple slices that can be consumed independently.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of <field>:<direction> pairs.

parameters.name: source
parameters.summary: The source fields that are returned for matching documents. These fields are returned in the `hits._source` property of the search response. If the `stored_fields` property is specified, the `_source` property defaults to `false`. Otherwise, it defaults to `true`.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.summary: An array of wildcard (`*`) field patterns. The request returns values for field names matching these patterns in the `hits.fields` property of the response.

parameters.name: suggest
parameters.summary: Defines a suggester that provides similar looking terms based on a provided text.

parameters.name: terminate-after
parameters.summary: The maximum number of documents to collect for each shard. If a query reaches this limit, Elasticsearch terminates the query early. Elasticsearch collects documents before sorting. IMPORTANT: Use with caution. Elasticsearch applies this property to each shard handling the request. When possible, let Elasticsearch perform early termination automatically. Avoid specifying this property for requests that target data streams with backing indices across multiple data tiers. If set to `0` (default), the query does not terminate early.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: The period of time to wait for a response from each shard. If no response is received before the timeout expires, the request fails and returns an error. Defaults to no timeout.

parameters.name: track-scores
parameters.summary: If `true`, calculate and return document scores, even if the scores are not used for sorting.

parameters.name: version
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request returns the document version as part of a hit.

parameters.name: seq-no-primary-term
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request returns sequence number and primary term of the last modification of each hit.

parameters.name: stored-fields
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of stored fields to return as part of a hit. If no fields are specified, no stored fields are included in the response. If this field is specified, the `_source` property defaults to `false`. You can pass `_source: true` to return both source fields and stored fields in the search response.

parameters.name: pit
parameters.summary: Limit the search to a point in time (PIT). If you provide a PIT, you cannot specify an `<index>` in the request path.

parameters.name: runtime-mappings
parameters.summary: One or more runtime fields in the search request. These fields take precedence over mapped fields with the same name.

parameters.name: stats
parameters.summary: The stats groups to associate with the search. Each group maintains a statistics aggregation for its associated searches. You can retrieve these stats using the indices stats API.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: A list of indices, data streams, or aliases to search. It supports wildcards (`*`). To search all data streams and indices, omit this parameter or use `*` or `_all`. To search a remote cluster, use the `<cluster>:<target>` syntax.

parameters.name: field
parameters.summary: A field that contains the geospatial data to return. It must be a `geo_point` or `geo_shape` field. The field must have doc values enabled. It cannot be a nested field. NOTE: Vector tiles do not natively support geometry collections. For `geometrycollection` values in a `geo_shape` field, the API returns a hits layer feature for each element of the collection. This behavior may change in a future release.

parameters.name: zoom
parameters.summary: The zoom level of the vector tile to search. It accepts `0` to `29`.

parameters.name: x
parameters.summary: The X coordinate for the vector tile to search.

parameters.name: y
parameters.summary: The Y coordinate for the vector tile to search.

parameters.name: aggs
parameters.summary: Sub-aggregations for the geotile_grid. It supports the following aggregation types: - `avg` - `boxplot` - `cardinality` - `extended stats` - `max` - `median absolute deviation` - `min` - `percentile` - `percentile-rank` - `stats` - `sum` - `value count` The aggregation names can't start with `_mvt_`. The `_mvt_` prefix is reserved for internal aggregations.

parameters.name: buffer
parameters.summary: The size, in pixels, of a clipping buffer outside the tile. This allows renderers to avoid outline artifacts from geometries that extend past the extent of the tile.

parameters.name: exact-bounds
parameters.summary: If `false`, the meta layer's feature is the bounding box of the tile. If `true`, the meta layer's feature is a bounding box resulting from a `geo_bounds` aggregation. The aggregation runs on <field> values that intersect the `<zoom>/<x>/<y>` tile with `wrap_longitude` set to `false`. The resulting bounding box may be larger than the vector tile.

parameters.name: extent
parameters.summary: The size, in pixels, of a side of the tile. Vector tiles are square with equal sides.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.summary: The fields to return in the `hits` layer. It supports wildcards (`*`). This parameter does not support fields with array values. Fields with array values may return inconsistent results.

parameters.name: grid-agg
parameters.summary: The aggregation used to create a grid for the `field`.

parameters.name: grid-precision
parameters.summary: Additional zoom levels available through the aggs layer. For example, if `<zoom>` is `7` and `grid_precision` is `8`, you can zoom in up to level 15. Accepts 0-8. If 0, results don't include the aggs layer.

parameters.name: grid-type
parameters.summary: Determines the geometry type for features in the aggs layer. In the aggs layer, each feature represents a `geotile_grid` cell. If `grid, each feature is a polygon of the cells bounding box. If `point`, each feature is a Point that is the centroid of the cell.

parameters.name: query
parameters.summary: The query DSL used to filter documents for the search.

parameters.name: runtime-mappings
parameters.summary: Defines one or more runtime fields in the search request. These fields take precedence over mapped fields with the same name.

parameters.name: size
parameters.summary: The maximum number of features to return in the hits layer. Accepts 0-10000. If 0, results don't include the hits layer.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.summary: Sort the features in the hits layer. By default, the API calculates a bounding box for each feature. It sorts features based on this box's diagonal length, from longest to shortest.

parameters.name: track-total-hits
parameters.summary: The number of hits matching the query to count accurately. If `true`, the exact number of hits is returned at the cost of some performance. If `false`, the response does not include the total number of hits matching the query.

parameters.name: with-labels
parameters.summary: If `true`, the hits and aggs layers will contain additional point features representing suggested label positions for the original features. * `Point` and `MultiPoint` features will have one of the points selected. * `Polygon` and `MultiPolygon` features will have a single point generated, either the centroid, if it is within the polygon, or another point within the polygon selected from the sorted triangle-tree. * `LineString` features will likewise provide a roughly central point selected from the triangle-tree. * The aggregation results will provide one central point for each aggregation bucket. All attributes from the original features will also be copied to the new label features. In addition, the new features will be distinguishable using the tag `_mvt_label_position`.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of data streams, indices, and aliases to search. It supports wildcards (`*`). To search all data streams and indices, omit this parameter or use `*` or `_all`.

parameters.name: allow-no-indices
parameters.summary: A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression. If `false`, the request returns an error (1) if any wildcard expression (including `_all` and `*`) resolves to zero matching indices or (2) if the complete set of resolved indices, aliases or data streams is empty after all expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.name: expand-wildcards
parameters.summary: Type of index that wildcard patterns can match. If the request can target data streams, this argument determines whether wildcard expressions match hidden data streams. Supports comma-separated values, such as `open,hidden`.

parameters.name: ignore-unavailable
parameters.summary: If `false`, the request returns an error if it targets a concrete (non-wildcarded) index, alias, or data stream that is missing, closed, or otherwise unavailable. If `true`, unavailable concrete targets are silently ignored.

parameters.name: local
parameters.summary: If `true`, the request retrieves information from the local node only.

parameters.name: master-timeout
parameters.summary: The period to wait for a connection to the master node. If the master node is not available before the timeout expires, the request fails and returns an error. IT can also be set to `-1` to indicate that the request should never timeout.

parameters.name: preference
parameters.summary: The node or shard the operation should be performed on. It is random by default.

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: A custom value used to route operations to a specific shard.

parameters.name: allow-no-indices

If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.name: ccs-minimize-roundtrips
parameters.summary: Indicates whether network round-trips should be minimized as part of cross-cluster search requests execution.

parameters.name: expand-wildcards

parameters.name: ignore-throttled
parameters.summary: If `true`, specified concrete, expanded, or aliased indices are not included in the response when throttled.

parameters.name: ignore-unavailable

parameters.name: routing

parameters.name: scroll

parameters.name: search-type

parameters.name: rest-total-hits-as-int

parameters.name: typed-keys

parameters.name: explain

parameters.name: id

parameters.name: params

parameters.name: profile

parameters.name: source
parameters.summary: An inline search template. Supports the same parameters as the search API's request body. It also supports Mustache variables. If no `id` is specified, this parameter is required.

parameters.summary: A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.name: index-filter
parameters.summary: Filter indices if the provided query rewrites to `match_none` on every shard. IMPORTANT: The filtering is done on a best-effort basis, it uses index statistics and mappings to rewrite queries to `match_none` instead of fully running the request. For instance a range query over a date field can rewrite to `match_none` if all documents within a shard (including deleted documents) are outside of the provided range. However, not all queries can rewrite to `match_none` so this API may return an index even if the provided filter matches no document.

parameters.name: runtime-mappings
parameters.summary: Define ad-hoc runtime fields in the request similar to the way it is done in search requests. These fields exist only as part of the query and take precedence over fields defined with the same name in the index mappings.

parameters.name: timeout

parameters.name: case-insensitive

parameters.name: search-after
parameters.summary: The string after which terms in the index should be returned. It allows for a form of pagination if the last result from one request is passed as the `search_after` parameter for a subsequent request.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.summary: Filter terms based on their tf-idf scores. This could be useful in order find out a good characteristic vector of a document. This feature works in a similar manner to the second phase of the More Like This Query.

parameters.name: per-field-analyzer
parameters.summary: Override the default per-field analyzer. This is useful in order to generate term vectors in any fashion, especially when using artificial documents. When providing an analyzer for a field that already stores term vectors, the term vectors will be regenerated.

parameters.name: fields

parameters.name: field-statistics
parameters.summary: If `true`, the response includes: * The document count (how many documents contain this field). * The sum of document frequencies (the sum of document frequencies for all terms in this field). * The sum of total term frequencies (the sum of total term frequencies of each term in this field).

parameters.name: offsets

parameters.name: payloads

parameters.name: positions

parameters.name: term-statistics

By default these values are not returned since term statistics can have a serious performance impact.

parameters.summary: Specifies to wait for the search to be completed up until the provided timeout. Final results will be returned if available before the timeout expires, otherwise the currently available results will be returned once the timeout expires. By default no timeout is set meaning that the currently available results will be returned without any additional wait.

parameters.name: return-intermediate-results
parameters.summary: Specifies whether the response should contain intermediate results if the query is still running when the wait_for_completion_timeout expires or if no wait_for_completion_timeout is specified. If true and the search is still running, the search response will include any hits and partial aggregations that are available. If false and the search is still running, the search response will not include any hits (but possibly include total hits) nor will include any partial aggregations. When not specified, the intermediate results are returned for running queries.

parameters.name: docvalue-fields
parameters.summary: Array of wildcard (*) patterns. The request returns doc values for field names matching these patterns in the hits.fields property of the response.

parameters.name: knn
parameters.summary: Defines the approximate kNN search to run.

parameters.name: min-score
parameters.summary: Minimum _score for matching documents. Documents with a lower _score are not included in search results and results collected by aggregations.

parameters.name: query
parameters.summary: Defines the search definition using the Query DSL.

parameters.name: script-fields
parameters.summary: Retrieve a script evaluation (based on different fields) for each hit.

parameters.name: size
parameters.summary: The number of hits to return. By default, you cannot page through more than 10,000 hits using the from and size parameters. To page through more hits, use the search_after parameter.

parameters.name: source
parameters.summary: Indicates which source fields are returned for matching documents. These fields are returned in the hits._source property of the search response.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.summary: Array of wildcard (*) patterns. The request returns values for field names matching these patterns in the hits.fields property of the response.

parameters.name: terminate-after
parameters.summary: Maximum number of documents to collect for each shard. If a query reaches this limit, Elasticsearch terminates the query early. Elasticsearch collects documents before sorting. Defaults to 0, which does not terminate query execution early.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: Specifies the period of time to wait for a response from each shard. If no response is received before the timeout expires, the request fails and returns an error. Defaults to no timeout.

parameters.name: track-scores
parameters.summary: If true, calculate and return document scores, even if the scores are not used for sorting.

parameters.name: version
parameters.summary: If true, returns document version as part of a hit.

parameters.name: seq-no-primary-term
parameters.summary: If true, returns sequence number and primary term of the last modification of each hit. See Optimistic concurrency control.

parameters.name: stored-fields
parameters.summary: List of stored fields to return as part of a hit. If no fields are specified, no stored fields are included in the response. If this field is specified, the _source parameter defaults to false. You can pass _source: true to return both source fields and stored fields in the search response.

parameters.name: pit
parameters.summary: Limits the search to a point in time (PIT). If you provide a PIT, you cannot specify an <index> in the request path.

parameters.name: runtime-mappings
parameters.summary: Defines one or more runtime fields in the search request. These fields take precedence over mapped fields with the same name.

parameters.name: stats
parameters.summary: Stats groups to associate with the search. Each group maintains a statistics aggregation for its associated searches. You can retrieve these stats using the indices stats API.

parameters.name: name
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of aliases to retrieve. Supports wildcards (`*`).  To retrieve all aliases, omit this parameter or use `*` or `_all`.

parameters.name: h
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of columns names to display. It supports simple wildcards.

parameters.name: s
parameters.summary: List of columns that determine how the table should be sorted. Sorting defaults to ascending and can be changed by setting `:asc` or `:desc` as a suffix to the column name.

parameters.name: expand-wildcards
parameters.summary: The type of index that wildcard patterns can match. If the request can target data streams, this argument determines whether wildcard expressions match hidden data streams. It supports comma-separated values, such as `open,hidden`.

parameters.name: master-timeout
parameters.summary: The period to wait for a connection to the master node. If the master node is not available before the timeout expires, the request fails and returns an error. To indicated that the request should never timeout, you can set it to `-1`.

parameters.name: node-id
parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of node identifiers or names used to limit the returned information.

parameters.summary: A comma-separated list of columns names to display. It supports simple wildcards.

parameters.name: s
parameters.summary: List of columns that determine how the table should be sorted. Sorting defaults to ascending and can be changed by setting `:asc` or `:desc` as a suffix to the column name.

parameters.summary: A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.summary: The maximum number of operations to pull per read from the remote cluster.

parameters.name: max-read-request-size
parameters.summary: The maximum size in bytes of per read of a batch of operations pulled from the remote cluster.

parameters.name: max-retry-delay
parameters.summary: The maximum time to wait before retrying an operation that failed exceptionally. An exponential backoff strategy is employed when retrying.

parameters.name: max-write-buffer-count
parameters.summary: The maximum number of operations that can be queued for writing. When this limit is reached, reads from the remote cluster will be deferred until the number of queued operations goes below the limit.

parameters.name: max-write-buffer-size
parameters.summary: The maximum total bytes of operations that can be queued for writing. When this limit is reached, reads from the remote cluster will be deferred until the total bytes of queued operations goes below the limit.

parameters.name: max-write-request-operation-count
parameters.summary: The maximum number of operations per bulk write request executed on the follower.

parameters.name: max-write-request-size
parameters.summary: The maximum total bytes of operations per bulk write request executed on the follower.

parameters.name: read-poll-timeout
parameters.summary: The maximum time to wait for new operations on the remote cluster when the follower index is synchronized with the leader index. When the timeout has elapsed, the poll for operations will return to the follower so that it can update some statistics. Then the follower will immediately attempt to read from the leader again.

parameters.name: name
parameters.summary: Name of the component template to create. Elasticsearch includes the following built-in component templates: `logs-mappings`; `logs-settings`; `metrics-mappings`; `metrics-settings`;`synthetics-mapping`; `synthetics-settings`. Elastic Agent uses these templates to configure backing indices for its data streams. If you use Elastic Agent and want to overwrite one of these templates, set the `version` for your replacement template higher than the current version. If you don’t use Elastic Agent and want to disable all built-in component and index templates, set `stack.templates.enabled` to `false` using the cluster update settings API.

parameters.name: keep-alive

parameters.name: wait-for-completion-timeout
parameters.summary: Timeout duration to wait for the request to finish. Defaults to no timeout, meaning the request waits for complete search results.
