parameters.summary: Comma-separated list of index names to scope the operation

parameters.name: allow-no-indices
parameters.summary: A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression. If `false`, the request returns an error (1) if any wildcard expression (including `_all` and `*`) resolves to zero matching indices or (2) if the complete set of resolved indices, aliases or data streams is empty after all expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.name: expand-wildcards
parameters.summary: Whether to expand wildcard expression to concrete indices that are open, closed or both.

parameters.name: ccs-minimize-roundtrips
parameters.summary: Indicates whether network round-trips should be minimized as part of cross-cluster search requests execution

parameters.name: ignore-unavailable
parameters.summary: If `false`, the request returns an error if it targets a concrete (non-wildcarded) index, alias, or data stream that is missing, closed, or otherwise unavailable. If `true`, unavailable concrete targets are silently ignored.

parameters.name: query
parameters.summary: EQL query you wish to run.

parameters.name: event-category-field
parameters.summary: Field containing the event classification, such as process, file, or network.

parameters.name: tiebreaker-field
parameters.summary: Field used to sort hits with the same timestamp in ascending order

parameters.name: timestamp-field
parameters.summary: Field containing event timestamp.

parameters.name: fetch-size
parameters.summary: Maximum number of events to search at a time for sequence queries.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.summary: Query, written in Query DSL, used to filter the events on which the EQL query runs.

parameters.name: allow-partial-search-results
parameters.summary: Allow query execution also in case of shard failures. If true, the query will keep running and will return results based on the available shards. For sequences, the behavior can be further refined using allow_partial_sequence_results

parameters.name: allow-partial-sequence-results
parameters.summary: This flag applies only to sequences and has effect only if allow_partial_search_results=true. If true, the sequence query will return results based on the available shards, ignoring the others. If false, the sequence query will return successfully, but will always have empty results.

parameters.name: size
parameters.summary: For basic queries, the maximum number of matching events to return. Defaults to 10

parameters.name: fields
parameters.summary: Array of wildcard (*) patterns. The response returns values for field names matching these patterns in the fields property of each hit.

parameters.name: max-samples-per-key
parameters.summary: By default, the response of a sample query contains up to `10` samples, with one sample per unique set of join keys. Use the `size` parameter to get a smaller or larger set of samples. To retrieve more than one sample per set of join keys, use the `max_samples_per_key` parameter. Pipes are not supported for sample queries.

parameters.name: allow-partial-results
parameters.summary: If `true`, partial results will be returned if there are shard failures, but the query can continue to execute on other clusters and shards. If `false`, the query will fail if there are any failures. To override the default behavior, you can set the `esql.query.allow_partial_results` cluster setting to `false`.

parameters.name: delimiter
parameters.summary: The character to use between values within a CSV row. It is valid only for the CSV format.

parameters.name: drop-null-columns
parameters.summary: Indicates whether columns that are entirely `null` will be removed from the `columns` and `values` portion of the results. If `true`, the response will include an extra section under the name `all_columns` which has the name of all the columns.

parameters.name: format
parameters.summary: A short version of the Accept header, e.g. json, yaml. `csv`, `tsv`, and `txt` formats will return results in a tabular format, excluding other metadata fields from the response. For async requests, nothing will be returned if the async query doesn't finish within the timeout. The query ID and running status are available in the `X-Elasticsearch-Async-Id` and `X-Elasticsearch-Async-Is-Running` HTTP headers of the response, respectively.

parameters.name: columnar
parameters.summary: By default, ES|QL returns results as rows. For example, FROM returns each individual document as one row. For the JSON, YAML, CBOR and smile formats, ES|QL can return the results in a columnar fashion where one row represents all the values of a certain column in the results.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.summary: Specify a Query DSL query in the filter parameter to filter the set of documents that an ES|QL query runs on.

parameters.name: time-zone
parameters.summary: Sets the default timezone of the query.

parameters.name: locale
parameters.summary: Returns results (especially dates) formatted per the conventions of the locale.

parameters.name: params
parameters.summary: To avoid any attempts of hacking or code injection, extract the values in a separate list of parameters. Use question mark placeholders (?) in the query string for each of the parameters.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.summary: If provided and `true` the response will include an extra `profile` object with information on how the query was executed. This information is for human debugging and its format can change at any time but it can give some insight into the performance of each part of the query.

parameters.name: query
parameters.summary: The ES|QL query API accepts an ES|QL query string in the query parameter, runs it, and returns the results.

parameters.name: tables
parameters.summary: Tables to use with the LOOKUP operation. The top level key is the table name and the next level key is the column name.

parameters.name: include-ccs-metadata
parameters.summary: When set to `true` and performing a cross-cluster/cross-project query, the response will include an extra `_clusters` object with information about the clusters that participated in the search along with info such as shards count.

parameters.name: include-execution-metadata
parameters.summary: When set to `true`, the response will include an extra `_clusters` object with information about the clusters that participated in the search along with info such as shards count. This is similar to `include_ccs_metadata`, but it also returns metadata when the query is not CCS/CPS

parameters.name: wait-for-completion-timeout
parameters.summary: The period to wait for the request to finish. By default, the request waits for 1 second for the query results. If the query completes during this period, results are returned Otherwise, a query ID is returned that can later be used to retrieve the results.

parameters.name: keep-alive
parameters.summary: The period for which the query and its results are stored in the cluster. The default period is five days. When this period expires, the query and its results are deleted, even if the query is still ongoing. If the `keep_on_completion` parameter is false, Elasticsearch only stores async queries that do not complete within the period set by the `wait_for_completion_timeout` parameter, regardless of this value.

parameters.name: keep-on-completion
parameters.summary: Indicates whether the query and its results are stored in the cluster. If false, the query and its results are stored in the cluster only if the request does not complete during the period set by the `wait_for_completion_timeout` parameter.

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: The unique identifier of the query. A query ID is provided in the ES|QL async query API response for a query that does not complete in the designated time. A query ID is also provided when the request was submitted with the `keep_on_completion` parameter set to `true`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: The unique identifier of the query. A query ID is provided in the ES|QL async query API response for a query that does not complete in the designated time. A query ID is also provided when the request was submitted with the `keep_on_completion` parameter set to `true`.

parameters.name: drop-null-columns
parameters.summary: Indicates whether columns that are entirely `null` will be removed from the `columns` and `values` portion of the results. If `true`, the response will include an extra section under the name `all_columns` which has the name of all the columns.

parameters.name: format
parameters.summary: A short version of the Accept header, for example `json` or `yaml`.

parameters.name: keep-alive
parameters.summary: The period for which the query and its results are stored in the cluster. When this period expires, the query and its results are deleted, even if the query is still ongoing.

parameters.name: wait-for-completion-timeout
parameters.summary: The period to wait for the request to finish. By default, the request waits for complete query results. If the request completes during the period specified in this parameter, complete query results are returned. Otherwise, the response returns an `is_running` value of `true` and no results.

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: The unique identifier of the query. A query ID is provided in the ES|QL async query API response for a query that does not complete in the designated time. A query ID is also provided when the request was submitted with the `keep_on_completion` parameter set to `true`.

parameters.name: drop-null-columns
parameters.summary: Indicates whether columns that are entirely `null` will be removed from the `columns` and `values` portion of the results. If `true`, the response will include an extra section under the name `all_columns` which has the name of all the columns.

parameters.name: name
parameters.summary: The view name to remove.

parameters.name: query

parameters.name: format
parameters.summary: A short version of the Accept header, e.g. json, yaml. `csv`, `tsv`, and `txt` formats will return results in a tabular format, excluding other metadata fields from the response.

parameters.name: delimiter
parameters.summary: The character to use between values within a CSV row. Only valid for the CSV format.

parameters.name: drop-null-columns
parameters.summary: Should columns that are entirely `null` be removed from the `columns` and `values` portion of the results? Defaults to `false`. If `true` then the response will include an extra section under the name `all_columns` which has the name of all columns.

parameters.name: allow-partial-results
parameters.summary: If `true`, partial results will be returned if there are shard failures, but the query can continue to execute on other clusters and shards. If `false`, the query will fail if there are any failures. To override the default behavior, you can set the `esql.query.allow_partial_results` cluster setting to `false`.

parameters.name: columnar
parameters.summary: By default, ES|QL returns results as rows. For example, FROM returns each individual document as one row. For the JSON, YAML, CBOR and smile formats, ES|QL can return the results in a columnar fashion where one row represents all the values of a certain column in the results.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.summary: Specify a Query DSL query in the filter parameter to filter the set of documents that an ES|QL query runs on.

parameters.name: time-zone
parameters.summary: Sets the default timezone of the query.

parameters.name: locale
parameters.summary: Returns results (especially dates) formatted per the conventions of the locale.

parameters.name: params
parameters.summary: To avoid any attempts of hacking or code injection, extract the values in a separate list of parameters. Use question mark placeholders (?) in the query string for each of the parameters.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.summary: If provided and `true` the response will include an extra `profile` object with information on how the query was executed. This information is for human debugging and its format can change at any time but it can give some insight into the performance of each part of the query.

parameters.name: query
parameters.summary: The ES|QL query API accepts an ES|QL query string in the query parameter, runs it, and returns the results.

parameters.name: tables
parameters.summary: Tables to use with the LOOKUP operation. The top level key is the table name and the next level key is the column name.

parameters.name: include-ccs-metadata
parameters.summary: When set to `true` and performing a cross-cluster/cross-project query, the response will include an extra `_clusters` object with information about the clusters that participated in the search along with info such as shards count.

parameters.name: include-execution-metadata
parameters.summary: When set to `true`, the response will include an extra `_clusters` object with information about the clusters that participated in the search along with info such as shards count. This is similar to `include_ccs_metadata`, but it also returns metadata when the query is not CCS/CPS

parameters.name: wait-for-advance
parameters.summary: A boolean value which controls whether to wait (until the timeout) for the global checkpoints to advance past the provided `checkpoints`.

parameters.name: wait-for-index
parameters.summary: A boolean value which controls whether to wait (until the timeout) for the target index to exist and all primary shards be active. Can only be true when `wait_for_advance` is true.

parameters.name: checkpoints
parameters.summary: A comma separated list of previous global checkpoints. When used in combination with `wait_for_advance`, the API will only return once the global checkpoints advances past the checkpoints. Providing an empty list will cause Elasticsearch to immediately return the current global checkpoints.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: Period to wait for a global checkpoints to advance past `checkpoints`.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: A single target to search. If the target is an index alias, it must resolve to a single index.

parameters.name: allow-no-indices
parameters.summary: A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression. If `false`, the request returns an error (1) if any wildcard expression (including `_all` and `*`) resolves to zero matching indices or (2) if the complete set of resolved indices, aliases or data streams is empty after all expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.name: ccs-minimize-roundtrips
parameters.summary: If true, network roundtrips between the coordinating node and remote clusters are minimized for cross-cluster search requests.

parameters.name: expand-wildcards
parameters.summary: Type of index that wildcard expressions can match. If the request can target data streams, this argument determines whether wildcard expressions match hidden data streams.

parameters.name: ignore-throttled
parameters.summary: If true, concrete, expanded or aliased indices are ignored when frozen.

parameters.name: ignore-unavailable
parameters.summary: If `false`, the request returns an error if it targets a concrete (non-wildcarded) index, alias, or data stream that is missing, closed, or otherwise unavailable. If `true`, unavailable concrete targets are silently ignored.

parameters.name: max-concurrent-searches
parameters.summary: Maximum number of concurrent searches the multi search API can execute.

parameters.name: max-concurrent-shard-requests
parameters.summary: Maximum number of concurrent shard requests that each sub-search request executes per node.

parameters.name: pre-filter-shard-size
parameters.summary: Defines a threshold that enforces a pre-filter roundtrip to prefilter search shards based on query rewriting if the number of shards the search request expands to exceeds the threshold. This filter roundtrip can limit the number of shards significantly if for instance a shard can not match any documents based on its rewrite method i.e., if date filters are mandatory to match but the shard bounds and the query are disjoint.

parameters.name: search-type
parameters.summary: Indicates whether global term and document frequencies should be used when scoring returned documents.

parameters.name: rest-total-hits-as-int
parameters.summary: If true, hits.total are returned as an integer in the response. Defaults to false, which returns an object.

parameters.name: typed-keys
parameters.summary: Specifies whether aggregation and suggester names should be prefixed by their respective types in the response.

parameters.name: wait-for-checkpoints
parameters.summary: A comma separated list of checkpoints. When configured, the search API will only be executed on a shard after the relevant checkpoint has become visible for search. Defaults to an empty list which will cause Elasticsearch to immediately execute the search.

parameters.name: allow-partial-search-results
parameters.summary: If true, returns partial results if there are shard request timeouts or shard failures. If false, returns an error with no partial results. Defaults to the configured cluster setting `search.default_allow_partial_results`, which is true by default.

parameters.name: index
parameters.summary: A single target to search. If the target is an index alias, it must resolve to a single index.

parameters.name: allow-no-indices
parameters.summary: A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression. If `false`, the request returns an error (1) if any wildcard expression (including `_all` and `*`) resolves to zero matching indices or (2) if the complete set of resolved indices, aliases or data streams is empty after all expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.name: ignore-unavailable
parameters.summary: If `false`, the request returns an error if it targets a concrete (non-wildcarded) index, alias, or data stream that is missing, closed, or otherwise unavailable. If `true`, unavailable concrete targets are silently ignored.

parameters.name: suggest-field
parameters.summary: Specifies which field to use for suggestions.

parameters.name: suggest-text
parameters.summary: The source text for which the suggestions should be returned.

parameters.name: wait-for-checkpoints

parameters.name: explain
parameters.summary: If true, returns detailed information about score computation as part of a hit.

parameters.name: ext
parameters.summary: Configuration of search extensions defined by Elasticsearch plugins.

parameters.name: from
parameters.summary: Starting document offset. By default, you cannot page through more than 10,000 hits using the from and size parameters. To page through more hits, use the search_after parameter.

parameters.name: track-total-hits
parameters.summary: Number of hits matching the query to count accurately. If true, the exact number of hits is returned at the cost of some performance. If false, the response does not include the total number of hits matching the query. Defaults to 10,000 hits.

parameters.name: indices-boost
parameters.summary: Boosts the _score of documents from specified indices.

parameters.name: docvalue-fields
parameters.summary: Array of wildcard (*) patterns. The request returns doc values for field names matching these patterns in the hits.fields property of the response.

parameters.name: min-score
parameters.summary: Minimum _score for matching documents. Documents with a lower _score are not included in search results and results collected by aggregations.

parameters.name: query
parameters.summary: Defines the search definition using the Query DSL.

parameters.name: script-fields
parameters.summary: Retrieve a script evaluation (based on different fields) for each hit.

parameters.name: size
parameters.summary: The number of hits to return. By default, you cannot page through more than 10,000 hits using the from and size parameters. To page through more hits, use the search_after parameter.

parameters.name: source
parameters.summary: Indicates which source fields are returned for matching documents. These fields are returned in the hits._source property of the search response.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.summary: Array of wildcard (*) patterns. The request returns values for field names matching these patterns in the hits.fields property of the response.

parameters.name: terminate-after
parameters.summary: Maximum number of documents to collect for each shard. If a query reaches this limit, Elasticsearch terminates the query early. Elasticsearch collects documents before sorting. Defaults to 0, which does not terminate query execution early.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: Specifies the period of time to wait for a response from each shard. If no response is received before the timeout expires, the request fails and returns an error. Defaults to no timeout.

parameters.name: track-scores
parameters.summary: If true, calculate and return document scores, even if the scores are not used for sorting.

parameters.name: version
parameters.summary: If true, returns document version as part of a hit.

parameters.name: seq-no-primary-term
parameters.summary: If true, returns sequence number and primary term of the last modification of each hit. See Optimistic concurrency control.

parameters.name: stored-fields
parameters.summary: List of stored fields to return as part of a hit. If no fields are specified, no stored fields are included in the response. If this field is specified, the _source parameter defaults to false. You can pass _source: true to return both source fields and stored fields in the search response.

parameters.name: pit
parameters.summary: Limits the search to a point in time (PIT). If you provide a PIT, you cannot specify an <index> in the request path.

parameters.name: runtime-mappings
parameters.summary: Defines one or more runtime fields in the search request. These fields take precedence over mapped fields with the same name.

parameters.name: stats
parameters.summary: Stats groups to associate with the search. Each group maintains a statistics aggregation for its associated searches. You can retrieve these stats using the indices stats API.

parameters.name: index-routing
parameters.summary: Value used to route indexing operations to a specific shard. If specified, this overwrites the `routing` value for indexing operations. Data stream aliases don’t support this parameter.

parameters.name: is-write-index
parameters.summary: If `true`, sets the write index or data stream for the alias. If an alias points to multiple indices or data streams and `is_write_index` isn’t set, the alias rejects write requests. If an index alias points to one index and `is_write_index` isn’t set, the index automatically acts as the write index. Data stream aliases don’t automatically set a write data stream, even if the alias points to one data stream.

parameters.name: routing
parameters.summary: Value used to route indexing and search operations to a specific shard. Data stream aliases don’t support this parameter.

parameters.name: search-routing
parameters.summary: Value used to route search operations to a specific shard. If specified, this overwrites the `routing` value for search operations. Data stream aliases don’t support this parameter.

parameters.name: data-retention
parameters.summary: If defined, every document added to this data stream will be stored at least for this time frame. Any time after this duration the document could be deleted. When empty, every document in this data stream will be stored indefinitely.

parameters.name: downsampling
parameters.summary: The downsampling configuration to execute for the managed backing index after rollover.

parameters.name: downsampling-method
parameters.summary: The method used to downsample the data. There are two options `aggregate` and `last_value`. It requires `downsampling` to be defined. Defaults to `aggregate`.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.summary: If defined, it turns data stream lifecycle on/off (`true`/`false`) for this data stream. A data stream lifecycle that's disabled (enabled: `false`) will have no effect on the data stream.

commands.name: put-index-template

parameters.name: name
parameters.summary: Index or template name

parameters.name: create
parameters.summary: If `true`, this request cannot replace or update existing index templates.

parameters.name: master-timeout
parameters.summary: Period to wait for a connection to the master node. If no response is received before the timeout expires, the request fails and returns an error.

parameters.name: cause
parameters.summary: User defined reason for creating or updating the index template

parameters.name: index-patterns
parameters.summary: Array of wildcard (`*`) expressions used to match the names of data streams and indices during creation.

parameters.name: composed-of
parameters.summary: An ordered list of component template names. Component templates are merged in the order specified, meaning that the last component template specified has the highest precedence.

parameters.name: template
parameters.summary: Template to be applied. It may optionally include an `aliases`, `mappings`, or `settings` configuration.

parameters.name: data-stream
parameters.summary: If this object is included, the template is used to create data streams and their backing indices. Supports an empty object. Data streams require a matching index template with a `data_stream` object.

parameters.name: priority
parameters.summary: Priority to determine index template precedence when a new data stream or index is created. The index template with the highest priority is chosen. If no priority is specified the template is treated as though it is of priority 0 (lowest priority). This number is not automatically generated by Elasticsearch.

parameters.name: version
parameters.summary: Version number used to manage index templates externally. This number is not automatically generated by Elasticsearch. External systems can use these version numbers to simplify template management. To unset a version, replace the template without specifying one.

parameters.name: meta
parameters.summary: Optional user metadata about the index template. It may have any contents. It is not automatically generated or used by Elasticsearch. This user-defined object is stored in the cluster state, so keeping it short is preferable To unset the metadata, replace the template without specifying it.

parameters.name: allow-auto-create
parameters.summary: This setting overrides the value of the `action.auto_create_index` cluster setting. If set to `true` in a template, then indices can be automatically created using that template even if auto-creation of indices is disabled via `actions.auto_create_index`. parameters.summary: The text on which you want to perform the inference task. It can be a single string or an array. > info > Inference endpoints for the `completion` task type currently only support a single string as input.

parameters.name: input-type
parameters.summary: Specifies the input data type for the embedding model. The `input_type` parameter only applies to Inference Endpoints with the `embedding` or `text_embedding` task type. Possible values include: * `SEARCH` * `INGEST` * `CLASSIFICATION` * `CLUSTERING` Not all services support all values. Unsupported values will trigger a validation exception. Accepted values depend on the configured inference service, refer to the relevant service-specific documentation for more info. > info > The `input_type` parameter specified on the root level of the request body will take precedence over the `input_type` parameter specified in `task_settings`.

Elasticsearch will not attempt to run the pipeline's remaining processors.

parameters.name: max-bucket-cardinality
parameters.summary: Estimates of the highest cardinality in a single bucket that is observed for influencer fields over the time period that the job analyzes data. To produce a good answer, values must be provided for all influencer fields. Providing values for fields that are not listed as `influencers` has no effect on the estimation.

parameters.name: overall-cardinality
parameters.summary: Estimates of the cardinality that is observed for fields over the whole time period that the job analyzes data. To produce a good answer, values must be provided for fields referenced in the `by_field_name`, `over_field_name` and `partition_field_name` of any detectors. Providing values for other fields has no effect on the estimation. It can be omitted from the request if no detectors have a `by_field_name`, `over_field_name` or `partition_field_name`.

parameters.summary: The approximate maximum amount of memory resources that are permitted for analytical processing. parameters.name: max-num-threads
parameters.summary: The maximum number of threads to be used by the analysis. Using more threads may decrease the time necessary to complete the analysis at the cost of using more CPU. Note that the process may use additional threads for operational functionality other than the analysis itself.

parameters.name: analyzed-fields
parameters.summary: Specify includes and/or excludes patterns to select which fields will be included in the analysis. The patterns specified in excludes are applied last, therefore excludes takes precedence. In other words, if the same field is specified in both includes and excludes, then the field will not be included in the analysis.

parameters.summary: Specifies whether the included model definition should be returned as a JSON map (true) or in a custom compressed format (false).

parameters.name: exclude-generated
parameters.summary: Indicates if certain fields should be removed from the configuration on retrieval. This allows the configuration to be in an acceptable format to be retrieved and then added to another cluster.

parameters.name: from
parameters.summary: Skips the specified number of models.

parameters.name: include
parameters.summary: A comma delimited string of optional fields to include in the response body.

parameters.name: size
parameters.summary: Specifies the maximum number of models to obtain.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.summary: A comma delimited string of tags. A trained model can have many tags, or none. When supplied, only trained models that contain all the supplied tags are returned.

parameters.name: model-id
parameters.summary: The unique identifier of the trained model or a model alias. It can be a comma-separated list or a wildcard expression.

parameters.name: allow-no-match
parameters.summary: Specifies what to do when the request: - Contains wildcard expressions and there are no models that match. - Contains the _all string or no identifiers and there are no matches. - Contains wildcard expressions and there are only partial matches. If true, it returns an empty array when there are no matches and the subset of results when there are partial matches.

parameters.name: from
parameters.summary: Skips the specified number of models.

parameters.name: size
parameters.summary: Specifies the maximum number of models to obtain.

parameters.name: model-id
parameters.summary: The unique identifier of the trained model.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: Controls the amount of time to wait for inference results.

parameters.name: docs
parameters.summary: An array of objects to pass to the model for inference. The objects should contain a fields matching your configured trained model input. Typically, for NLP models, the field name is `text_field`. Currently, for NLP models, only a single value is allowed.

parameters.name: inference-config
parameters.summary: The inference configuration updates to apply on the API call

parameters.name: job-id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the anomaly detection job.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: Refer to the description for the `timeout` query parameter.

commands.name: post-calendar-events

parameters.name: calendar-id
parameters.summary: A string that uniquely identifies a calendar.

parameters.name: events
parameters.summary: A list of one of more scheduled events. The event’s start and end times can be specified as integer milliseconds since the epoch or as a string in ISO 8601 format.

commands.summary: Add scheduled events to the calendar.

commands.name: post-data

parameters.name: job-id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the anomaly detection job. The job must have a state of open to receive and process the data.

parameters.name: reset-end
parameters.summary: Specifies the end of the bucket resetting range.

parameters.name: reset-start
parameters.summary: Specifies the start of the bucket resetting range.

commands.summary: Send data to an anomaly detection job for analysis.

commands.name: preview-data-frame-analytics

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the data frame analytics job.

parameters.name: config
parameters.summary: A data frame analytics config as described in create data frame analytics jobs. Note that `id` and `dest` don’t need to be provided in the context of this API.

commands.summary: Preview features used by data frame analytics.

parameters.name: datafeed-id
parameters.summary: A numerical character string that uniquely identifies the datafeed. This identifier can contain lowercase alphanumeric characters (a-z and 0-9), hyphens, and underscores. It must start and end with alphanumeric characters. NOTE: If you use this path parameter, you cannot provide datafeed or anomaly detection job configuration details in the request body.

parameters.name: start
parameters.summary: The start time from where the datafeed preview should begin

parameters.name: end
parameters.summary: The end time when the datafeed preview should stop

parameters.name: datafeed-config
parameters.summary: The datafeed definition to preview.

parameters.name: job-config
parameters.summary: The configuration details for the anomaly detection job that is associated with the datafeed. If the `datafeed_config` object does not include a `job_id` that references an existing anomaly detection job, you must supply this `job_config` object. If you include both a `job_id` and a `job_config`, the latter information is used. You cannot specify a `job_config` object unless you also supply a `datafeed_config` object.

parameters.name: calendar-id
parameters.summary: A string that uniquely identifies a calendar.

parameters.name: job-ids
parameters.summary: An array of anomaly detection job identifiers.

parameters.name: description
parameters.summary: A description of the calendar.

commands.name: put-calendar-job

parameters.name: calendar-id
parameters.summary: A string that uniquely identifies a calendar.

parameters.name: job-id
parameters.summary: An identifier for the anomaly detection jobs. It can be a job identifier, a group name, or a comma-separated list of jobs or groups.

commands.summary: Add anomaly detection job to calendar.

commands.name: put-data-frame-analytics

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the data frame analytics job. This identifier can contain lowercase alphanumeric characters (a-z and 0-9), hyphens, and underscores. It must start and end with alphanumeric characters.

parameters.name: allow-lazy-start
parameters.summary: Specifies whether this job can start when there is insufficient machine learning node capacity for it to be immediately assigned to a node. If set to `false` and a machine learning node with capacity to run the job cannot be immediately found, the API returns an error. If set to `true`, the API does not return an error; the job waits in the `starting` state until sufficient machine learning node capacity is available. This behavior is also affected by the cluster-wide `xpack.ml.max_lazy_ml_nodes` setting.

parameters.name: analysis
parameters.summary: The analysis configuration, which contains the information necessary to perform one of the following types of analysis: classification, outlier detection, or regression.

parameters.name: analyzed-fields
parameters.summary: Specifies `includes` and/or `excludes` patterns to select which fields will be included in the analysis. The patterns specified in `excludes` are applied last, therefore `excludes` takes precedence. In other words, if the same field is specified in both `includes` and `excludes`, then the field will not be included in the analysis. If `analyzed_fields` is not set, only the relevant fields will be included. For example, all the numeric fields for outlier detection. The supported fields vary for each type of analysis. Outlier detection requires numeric or `boolean` data to analyze. The algorithms don’t support missing values therefore fields that have data types other than numeric or boolean are ignored. Documents where included fields contain missing values, null values, or an array are also ignored. Therefore the `dest` index may contain documents that don’t have an outlier score. Regression supports fields that are numeric, `boolean`, `text`, `keyword`, and `ip` data types. It is also tolerant of missing values. Fields that are supported are included in the analysis, other fields are ignored. Documents where included fields contain an array with two or more values are also ignored. Documents in the `dest` index that don’t contain a results field are not included in the regression analysis. Classification supports fields that are numeric, `boolean`, `text`, `keyword`, and `ip` data types. It is also tolerant of missing values. Fields that are supported are included in the analysis, other fields are ignored. Documents where included fields contain an array with two or more values are also ignored. Documents in the `dest` index that don’t contain a results field are not included in the classification analysis. Classification analysis can be improved by mapping ordinal variable values to a single number. For example, in case of age ranges, you can model the values as `0-14 = 0`, `15-24 = 1`, `25-34 = 2`, and so on.

parameters.name: description
parameters.summary: A description of the job.

parameters.name: dest
parameters.summary: The destination configuration.

parameters.name: max-num-threads
parameters.summary: The maximum number of threads to be used by the analysis. Using more threads may decrease the time necessary to complete the analysis at the cost of using more CPU. Note that the process may use additional threads for operational functionality other than the analysis itself.

parameters.name: model-memory-limit
parameters.summary: The approximate maximum amount of memory resources that are permitted for analytical processing. If your `elasticsearch.yml` file contains an `xpack.ml.max_model_memory_limit` setting, an error occurs when you try to create data frame analytics jobs that have `model_memory_limit` values greater than that setting.

parameters.name: source
parameters.summary: The configuration of how to source the analysis data.

commands.summary: Create a data frame analytics job.

parameters.name: datafeed-id
parameters.summary: A numerical character string that uniquely identifies the datafeed. This identifier can contain lowercase alphanumeric characters (a-z and 0-9), hyphens, and underscores. It must start and end with alphanumeric characters.

parameters.name: allow-no-indices
parameters.summary: A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression. If `false`, the request returns an error (1) if any wildcard expression (including `_all` and `*`) resolves to zero matching indices or (2) if the complete set of resolved indices, aliases or data streams is empty after all expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.name: expand-wildcards
parameters.summary: Type of index that wildcard patterns can match. If the request can target data streams, this argument determines whether wildcard expressions match hidden data streams. Supports comma-separated values.

parameters.name: ignore-throttled
parameters.summary: If true, concrete, expanded, or aliased indices are ignored when frozen.

parameters.name: ignore-unavailable
parameters.summary: If `false`, the request returns an error if it targets a concrete (non-wildcarded) index, alias, or data stream that is missing, closed, or otherwise unavailable. If `true`, unavailable concrete targets are silently ignored.

parameters.name: aggregations
parameters.summary: If set, the datafeed performs aggregation searches. Support for aggregations is limited and should be used only with low cardinality data.

parameters.name: aggs
parameters.summary: If set, the datafeed performs aggregation searches. Support for aggregations is limited and should be used only with low cardinality data.

parameters.name: chunking-config
parameters.summary: Datafeeds might be required to search over long time periods, for several months or years. This search is split into time chunks in order to ensure the load on Elasticsearch is managed. Chunking configuration controls how the size of these time chunks are calculated; it is an advanced configuration option.

parameters.name: delayed-data-check-config
parameters.summary: Specifies whether the datafeed checks for missing data and the size of the window. The datafeed can optionally search over indices that have already been read in an effort to determine whether any data has subsequently been added to the index. If missing data is found, it is a good indication that the `query_delay` is set too low and the data is being indexed after the datafeed has passed that moment in time. This check runs only on real-time datafeeds.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.summary: The interval at which scheduled queries are made while the datafeed runs in real time. The default value is either the bucket span for short bucket spans, or, for longer bucket spans, a sensible fraction of the bucket span. When `frequency` is shorter than the bucket span, interim results for the last (partial) bucket are written then eventually overwritten by the full bucket results. If the datafeed uses aggregations, this value must be divisible by the interval of the date histogram aggregation.

parameters.name: indices
parameters.summary: An array of index names. Wildcards are supported. If any of the indices are in remote clusters, the master nodes and the machine learning nodes must have the `remote_cluster_client` role.

parameters.name: indexes
parameters.summary: An array of index names. Wildcards are supported. If any of the indices are in remote clusters, the master nodes and the machine learning nodes must have the `remote_cluster_client` role.

parameters.name: indices-options
parameters.summary: Specifies index expansion options that are used during search

parameters.name: job-id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the anomaly detection job.

parameters.name: max-empty-searches
parameters.summary: If a real-time datafeed has never seen any data (including during any initial training period), it automatically stops and closes the associated job after this many real-time searches return no documents. In other words, it stops after `frequency` times `max_empty_searches` of real-time operation. If not set, a datafeed with no end time that sees no data remains started until it is explicitly stopped. By default, it is not set.

parameters.name: query
parameters.summary: The Elasticsearch query domain-specific language (DSL). This value corresponds to the query object in an Elasticsearch search POST body. All the options that are supported by Elasticsearch can be used, as this object is passed verbatim to Elasticsearch.

parameters.name: query-delay
parameters.summary: The number of seconds behind real time that data is queried. For example, if data from 10:04 a.m. might not be searchable in Elasticsearch until 10:06 a.m., set this property to 120 seconds. The default value is randomly selected between `60s` and `120s`. This randomness improves the query performance when there are multiple jobs running on the same node.

parameters.name: runtime-mappings
parameters.summary: Specifies runtime fields for the datafeed search.

parameters.name: script-fields
parameters.summary: Specifies scripts that evaluate custom expressions and returns script fields to the datafeed. The detector configuration objects in a job can contain functions that use these script fields.

parameters.name: scroll-size
parameters.summary: The size parameter that is used in Elasticsearch searches when the datafeed does not use aggregations. The maximum value is the value of `index.max_result_window`, which is 10,000 by default.

parameters.name: filter-id
parameters.summary: A string that uniquely identifies a filter.

parameters.name: description
parameters.summary: A description of the filter.

parameters.name: items
parameters.summary: The items of the filter. A wildcard `*` can be used at the beginning or the end of an item. Up to 10000 items are allowed in each filter.

parameters.name: job-id
parameters.summary: The identifier for the anomaly detection job. This identifier can contain lowercase alphanumeric characters (a-z and 0-9), hyphens, and underscores. It must start and end with alphanumeric characters.

parameters.name: allow-no-indices
parameters.summary: A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression. If `false`, the request returns an error (1) if any wildcard expression (including `_all` and `*`) resolves to zero matching indices or (2) if the complete set of resolved indices, aliases or data streams is empty after all expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.name: expand-wildcards
parameters.summary: Type of index that wildcard patterns can match. If the request can target data streams, this argument determines whether wildcard expressions match hidden data streams. Supports comma-separated values.

parameters.name: ignore-throttled
parameters.summary: If `true`, concrete, expanded or aliased indices are ignored when frozen.

parameters.name: ignore-unavailable
parameters.summary: If `false`, the request returns an error if it targets a concrete (non-wildcarded) index, alias, or data stream that is missing, closed, or otherwise unavailable. If `true`, unavailable concrete targets are silently ignored.

parameters.name: allow-lazy-open
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option. Specifies whether this job can open when there is insufficient machine learning node capacity for it to be immediately assigned to a node. By default, if a machine learning node with capacity to run the job cannot immediately be found, the open anomaly detection jobs API returns an error. However, this is also subject to the cluster-wide `xpack.ml.max_lazy_ml_nodes` setting. If this option is set to true, the open anomaly detection jobs API does not return an error and the job waits in the opening state until sufficient machine learning node capacity is available.

parameters.name: analysis-config
parameters.summary: Specifies how to analyze the data. After you create a job, you cannot change the analysis configuration; all the properties are informational.

parameters.name: analysis-limits
parameters.summary: Limits can be applied for the resources required to hold the mathematical models in memory. These limits are approximate and can be set per job. They do not control the memory used by other processes, for example the Elasticsearch Java processes.

parameters.name: background-persist-interval
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option. The time between each periodic persistence of the model. The default value is a randomized value between 3 to 4 hours, which avoids all jobs persisting at exactly the same time. The smallest allowed value is 1 hour. For very large models (several GB), persistence could take 10-20 minutes, so do not set the `background_persist_interval` value too low.

parameters.name: custom-settings
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option. Contains custom meta data about the job.

parameters.name: daily-model-snapshot-retention-after-days
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option, which affects the automatic removal of old model snapshots for this job. It specifies a period of time (in days) after which only the first snapshot per day is retained. This period is relative to the timestamp of the most recent snapshot for this job. Valid values range from 0 to `model_snapshot_retention_days`.

parameters.name: data-description
parameters.summary: Defines the format of the input data when you send data to the job by using the post data API. Note that when configure a datafeed, these properties are automatically set. When data is received via the post data API, it is not stored in Elasticsearch. Only the results for anomaly detection are retained.

parameters.name: datafeed-config
parameters.summary: Defines a datafeed for the anomaly detection job. If Elasticsearch security features are enabled, your datafeed remembers which roles the user who created it had at the time of creation and runs the query using those same roles. If you provide secondary authorization headers, those credentials are used instead.

parameters.name: description
parameters.summary: A description of the job.

parameters.name: groups
parameters.summary: A list of job groups. A job can belong to no groups or many.

parameters.name: model-plot-config
parameters.summary: This advanced configuration option stores model information along with the results. It provides a more detailed view into anomaly detection. If you enable model plot it can add considerable overhead to the performance of the system; it is not feasible for jobs with many entities. Model plot provides a simplified and indicative view of the model and its bounds. It does not display complex features such as multivariate correlations or multimodal data. As such, anomalies may occasionally be reported which cannot be seen in the model plot. Model plot config can be configured when the job is created or updated later. It must be disabled if performance issues are experienced.

parameters.name: model-snapshot-retention-days
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option, which affects the automatic removal of old model snapshots for this job. It specifies the maximum period of time (in days) that snapshots are retained. This period is relative to the timestamp of the most recent snapshot for this job. By default, snapshots ten days older than the newest snapshot are deleted.

parameters.name: renormalization-window-days
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option. The period over which adjustments to the score are applied, as new data is seen. The default value is the longer of 30 days or 100 bucket spans.

parameters.name: results-index-name
parameters.summary: A text string that affects the name of the machine learning results index. By default, the job generates an index named `.ml-anomalies-shared`.

parameters.name: results-retention-days
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option. The period of time (in days) that results are retained. Age is calculated relative to the timestamp of the latest bucket result. If this property has a non-null value, once per day at 00:30 (server time), results that are the specified number of days older than the latest bucket result are deleted from Elasticsearch. The default value is null, which means all results are retained. Annotations generated by the system also count as results for retention purposes; they are deleted after the same number of days as results. Annotations added by users are retained forever.

parameters.name: model-id
parameters.summary: The unique identifier of the trained model.

parameters.name: defer-definition-decompression
parameters.summary: If set to `true` and a `compressed_definition` is provided, the request defers definition decompression and skips relevant validations.

parameters.name: wait-for-completion
parameters.summary: Whether to wait for all child operations (e.g. model download) to complete.

parameters.name: compressed-definition
parameters.summary: The compressed (GZipped and Base64 encoded) inference definition of the model. If compressed_definition is specified, then definition cannot be specified.

parameters.name: definition
parameters.summary: The inference definition for the model. If definition is specified, then compressed_definition cannot be specified.

parameters.name: description
parameters.summary: A human-readable description of the inference trained model.

parameters.name: inference-config
parameters.summary: The default configuration for inference. This can be either a regression or classification configuration. It must match the underlying definition.trained_model's target_type. For pre-packaged models such as ELSER the config is not required.

parameters.name: input
parameters.summary: The input field names for the model definition.

parameters.name: metadata
parameters.summary: An object map that contains metadata about the model.

parameters.name: model-type
parameters.summary: The model type.

parameters.name: model-size-bytes
parameters.summary: The estimated memory usage in bytes to keep the trained model in memory. This property is supported only if defer_definition_decompression is true or the model definition is not supplied.

parameters.name: platform-architecture
parameters.summary: The platform architecture (if applicable) of the trained mode. If the model only works on one platform, because it is heavily optimized for a particular processor architecture and OS combination, then this field specifies which. The format of the string must match the platform identifiers used by Elasticsearch, so one of, `linux-x86_64`, `linux-aarch64`, `darwin-x86_64`, `darwin-aarch64`, or `windows-x86_64`. For portable models (those that work independent of processor architecture or OS features), leave this field unset.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.summary: An array of tags to organize the model.

parameters.name: prefix-strings
parameters.summary: Optional prefix strings applied at inference

commands.name: put-trained-model-alias

parameters.name: model-alias
parameters.summary: The alias to create or update. This value cannot end in numbers.

parameters.name: model-id
parameters.summary: The identifier for the trained model that the alias refers to.

parameters.name: reassign
parameters.summary: Specifies whether the alias gets reassigned to the specified trained model if it is already assigned to a different model. If the alias is already assigned and this parameter is false, the API returns an error.

commands.summary: Create or update a trained model alias.

commands.name: put-trained-model-definition-part

parameters.name: model-id
parameters.summary: The unique identifier of the trained model.

parameters.name: part
parameters.summary: The definition part number. When the definition is loaded for inference the definition parts are streamed in the order of their part number. The first part must be `0` and the final part must be `total_parts - 1`.

parameters.name: definition
parameters.summary: The definition part for the model. Must be a base64 encoded string.

parameters.name: total-definition-length
parameters.summary: The total uncompressed definition length in bytes. Not base64 encoded.

parameters.name: total-parts
parameters.summary: The total number of parts that will be uploaded. Must be greater than 0.

commands.summary: Create part of a trained model definition.

parameters.name: model-id
parameters.summary: The unique identifier of the trained model.

parameters.name: vocabulary
parameters.summary: The model vocabulary, which must not be empty.

parameters.name: merges
parameters.summary: The optional model merges if required by the tokenizer.

parameters.name: scores
parameters.summary: The optional vocabulary value scores if required by the tokenizer.

parameters.name: job-id
parameters.summary: The ID of the job to reset.

parameters.name: wait-for-completion
parameters.summary: Should this request wait until the operation has completed before returning.

parameters.name: delete-user-annotations
parameters.summary: Specifies whether annotations that have been added by the user should be deleted along with any auto-generated annotations when the job is reset.

parameters.name: job-id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the anomaly detection job.

parameters.name: snapshot-id
parameters.summary: You can specify `empty` as the <snapshot_id>. Reverting to the empty snapshot means the anomaly detection job starts learning a new model from scratch when it is started.

parameters.name: delete-intervening-results
parameters.summary: Refer to the description for the `delete_intervening_results` query parameter.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.summary: When `true`, it enables `upgrade_mode` which temporarily halts all job and datafeed tasks and prohibits new job and datafeed tasks from starting.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: The time to wait for the request to be completed.

commands.name: start-data-frame-analytics

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the data frame analytics job. This identifier can contain lowercase alphanumeric characters (a-z and 0-9), hyphens, and underscores. It must start and end with alphanumeric characters.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: Controls the amount of time to wait until the data frame analytics job starts.

commands.summary: Start a data frame analytics job.

parameters.name: datafeed-id
parameters.summary: A numerical character string that uniquely identifies the datafeed. This identifier can contain lowercase alphanumeric characters (a-z and 0-9), hyphens, and underscores. It must start and end with alphanumeric characters.

parameters.name: end
parameters.summary: Refer to the description for the `end` query parameter.

parameters.name: start
parameters.summary: Refer to the description for the `start` query parameter.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: Refer to the description for the `timeout` query parameter.

parameters.name: model-id
parameters.summary: The unique identifier of the trained model. Currently, only PyTorch models are supported.

parameters.name: cache-size
parameters.summary: The inference cache size (in memory outside the JVM heap) per node for the model. The default value is the same size as the `model_size_bytes`. To disable the cache, `0b` can be provided.

parameters.name: number-of-allocations
parameters.summary: The number of model allocations on each node where the model is deployed. All allocations on a node share the same copy of the model in memory but use a separate set of threads to evaluate the model. Increasing this value generally increases the throughput. If this setting is greater than the number of hardware threads it will automatically be changed to a value less than the number of hardware threads. If adaptive_allocations is enabled, do not set this value, because it’s automatically set.

parameters.name: priority
parameters.summary: The deployment priority

parameters.name: queue-capacity
parameters.summary: Specifies the number of inference requests that are allowed in the queue. After the number of requests exceeds this value, new requests are rejected with a 429 error.

parameters.name: threads-per-allocation
parameters.summary: Sets the number of threads used by each model allocation during inference. This generally increases the inference speed. The inference process is a compute-bound process; any number greater than the number of available hardware threads on the machine does not increase the inference speed. If this setting is greater than the number of hardware threads it will automatically be changed to a value less than the number of hardware threads.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: Specifies the amount of time to wait for the model to deploy.

parameters.name: wait-for
parameters.summary: Specifies the allocation status to wait for before returning.

parameters.name: adaptive-allocations
parameters.summary: Adaptive allocations configuration. When enabled, the number of allocations is set based on the current load. If adaptive_allocations is enabled, do not set the number of allocations manually.

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the data frame analytics job. This identifier can contain lowercase alphanumeric characters (a-z and 0-9), hyphens, and underscores. It must start and end with alphanumeric characters.

parameters.name: allow-no-match
parameters.summary: Specifies what to do when the request: 1. Contains wildcard expressions and there are no data frame analytics jobs that match. 2. Contains the _all string or no identifiers and there are no matches. 3. Contains wildcard expressions and there are only partial matches. The default value is true, which returns an empty data_frame_analytics array when there are no matches and the subset of results when there are partial matches. If this parameter is false, the request returns a 404 status code when there are no matches or only partial matches.

parameters.name: force
parameters.summary: If true, the data frame analytics job is stopped forcefully.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: Controls the amount of time to wait until the data frame analytics job stops. Defaults to 20 seconds.

parameters.name: datafeed-id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the datafeed. You can stop multiple datafeeds in a single API request by using a comma-separated list of datafeeds or a wildcard expression. You can close all datafeeds by using `_all` or by specifying `*` as the identifier.

parameters.name: allow-no-match
parameters.summary: Refer to the description for the `allow_no_match` query parameter.

parameters.name: force
parameters.summary: Refer to the description for the `force` query parameter.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: Refer to the description for the `timeout` query parameter.

parameters.name: close-job
parameters.summary: Refer to the description for the `close_job` query parameter.

parameters.name: model-id
parameters.summary: The unique identifier of the trained model.

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: If provided, must be the same identifier as in the path.

parameters.name: allow-no-match
parameters.summary: Specifies what to do when the request: contains wildcard expressions and there are no deployments that match; contains the  `_all` string or no identifiers and there are no matches; or contains wildcard expressions and there are only partial matches. By default, it returns an empty array when there are no matches and the subset of results when there are partial matches. If `false`, the request returns a 404 status code when there are no matches or only partial matches.

parameters.name: force
parameters.summary: Forcefully stops the deployment, even if it is used by ingest pipelines. You can't use these pipelines until you restart the model deployment.

commands.name: update-data-frame-analytics

parameters.name: description
parameters.summary: A description of the job.

parameters.name: model-memory-limit
parameters.summary: The approximate maximum amount of memory resources that are permitted for analytical processing. If your `elasticsearch.yml` file contains an `xpack.ml.max_model_memory_limit` setting, an error occurs when you try to create data frame analytics jobs that have `model_memory_limit` values greater than that setting.

parameters.name: max-num-threads
parameters.summary: The maximum number of threads to be used by the analysis. Using more threads may decrease the time necessary to complete the analysis at the cost of using more CPU. Note that the process may use additional threads for operational functionality other than the analysis itself.

parameters.name: allow-lazy-start
parameters.summary: Specifies whether this job can start when there is insufficient machine learning node capacity for it to be immediately assigned to a node.

commands.summary: Update a data frame analytics job.

parameters.name: datafeed-id
parameters.summary: A numerical character string that uniquely identifies the datafeed. This identifier can contain lowercase alphanumeric characters (a-z and 0-9), hyphens, and underscores. It must start and end with alphanumeric characters.

parameters.name: allow-no-indices
parameters.summary: A setting that does two separate checks on the index expression. If `false`, the request returns an error (1) if any wildcard expression (including `_all` and `*`) resolves to zero matching indices or (2) if the complete set of resolved indices, aliases or data streams is empty after all expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve to no indices are allowed and the request returns an empty result.

parameters.name: expand-wildcards
parameters.summary: Type of index that wildcard patterns can match. If the request can target data streams, this argument determines whether wildcard expressions match hidden data streams. Supports comma-separated values.

parameters.name: ignore-throttled
parameters.summary: If `true`, concrete, expanded or aliased indices are ignored when frozen.

parameters.name: ignore-unavailable
parameters.summary: If `false`, the request returns an error if it targets a concrete (non-wildcarded) index, alias, or data stream that is missing, closed, or otherwise unavailable. If `true`, unavailable concrete targets are silently ignored.

parameters.name: aggregations
parameters.summary: If set, the datafeed performs aggregation searches. Support for aggregations is limited and should be used only with low cardinality data.

parameters.name: chunking-config
parameters.summary: Datafeeds might search over long time periods, for several months or years. This search is split into time chunks in order to ensure the load on Elasticsearch is managed. Chunking configuration controls how the size of these time chunks are calculated; it is an advanced configuration option.

parameters.name: delayed-data-check-config
parameters.summary: Specifies whether the datafeed checks for missing data and the size of the window. The datafeed can optionally search over indices that have already been read in an effort to determine whether any data has subsequently been added to the index. If missing data is found, it is a good indication that the `query_delay` is set too low and the data is being indexed after the datafeed has passed that moment in time. This check runs only on real-time datafeeds.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.summary: The interval at which scheduled queries are made while the datafeed runs in real time. The default value is either the bucket span for short bucket spans, or, for longer bucket spans, a sensible fraction of the bucket span. When `frequency` is shorter than the bucket span, interim results for the last (partial) bucket are written then eventually overwritten by the full bucket results. If the datafeed uses aggregations, this value must be divisible by the interval of the date histogram aggregation.

parameters.name: indices
parameters.summary: An array of index names. Wildcards are supported. If any of the indices are in remote clusters, the machine learning nodes must have the `remote_cluster_client` role.

parameters.name: indexes
parameters.summary: An array of index names. Wildcards are supported. If any of the indices are in remote clusters, the machine learning nodes must have the `remote_cluster_client` role.

parameters.name: indices-options
parameters.summary: Specifies index expansion options that are used during search.

parameters.name: max-empty-searches
parameters.summary: If a real-time datafeed has never seen any data (including during any initial training period), it automatically stops and closes the associated job after this many real-time searches return no documents. In other words, it stops after `frequency` times `max_empty_searches` of real-time operation. If not set, a datafeed with no end time that sees no data remains started until it is explicitly stopped. By default, it is not set.

parameters.name: query
parameters.summary: The Elasticsearch query domain-specific language (DSL). This value corresponds to the query object in an Elasticsearch search POST body. All the options that are supported by Elasticsearch can be used, as this object is passed verbatim to Elasticsearch. Note that if you change the query, the analyzed data is also changed. Therefore, the time required to learn might be long and the understandability of the results is unpredictable. If you want to make significant changes to the source data, it is recommended that you clone the job and datafeed and make the amendments in the clone. Let both run in parallel and close one when you are satisfied with the results of the job.

parameters.name: query-delay
parameters.summary: The number of seconds behind real time that data is queried. For example, if data from 10:04 a.m. might not be searchable in Elasticsearch until 10:06 a.m., set this property to 120 seconds. The default value is randomly selected between `60s` and `120s`. This randomness improves the query performance when there are multiple jobs running on the same node.

parameters.name: runtime-mappings
parameters.summary: Specifies runtime fields for the datafeed search.

parameters.name: script-fields
parameters.summary: Specifies scripts that evaluate custom expressions and returns script fields to the datafeed. The detector configuration objects in a job can contain functions that use these script fields.

parameters.name: scroll-size
parameters.summary: The size parameter that is used in Elasticsearch searches when the datafeed does not use aggregations. The maximum value is the value of `index.max_result_window`.

parameters.name: filter-id
parameters.summary: A string that uniquely identifies a filter.

parameters.name: add-items
parameters.summary: The items to add to the filter.

parameters.name: description
parameters.summary: A description for the filter.

parameters.name: remove-items
parameters.summary: The items to remove from the filter.

parameters.name: job-id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the job.

parameters.name: allow-lazy-open
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option. Specifies whether this job can open when there is insufficient machine learning node capacity for it to be immediately assigned to a node. If `false` and a machine learning node with capacity to run the job cannot immediately be found, the open anomaly detection jobs API returns an error. However, this is also subject to the cluster-wide `xpack.ml.max_lazy_ml_nodes` setting. If this option is set to `true`, the open anomaly detection jobs API does not return an error and the job waits in the opening state until sufficient machine learning node capacity is available.

parameters.name: background-persist-interval
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option. The time between each periodic persistence of the model. The default value is a randomized value between 3 to 4 hours, which avoids all jobs persisting at exactly the same time. The smallest allowed value is 1 hour. For very large models (several GB), persistence could take 10-20 minutes, so do not set the value too low. If the job is open when you make the update, you must stop the datafeed, close the job, then reopen the job and restart the datafeed for the changes to take effect.

parameters.name: custom-settings
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option. Contains custom meta data about the job. For example, it can contain custom URL information as shown in Adding custom URLs to machine learning results.

parameters.name: description
parameters.summary: A description of the job.

parameters.name: daily-model-snapshot-retention-after-days
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option, which affects the automatic removal of old model snapshots for this job. It specifies a period of time (in days) after which only the first snapshot per day is retained. This period is relative to the timestamp of the most recent snapshot for this job. Valid values range from 0 to `model_snapshot_retention_days`. For jobs created before version 7.8.0, the default value matches `model_snapshot_retention_days`.

parameters.name: model-snapshot-retention-days
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option, which affects the automatic removal of old model snapshots for this job. It specifies the maximum period of time (in days) that snapshots are retained. This period is relative to the timestamp of the most recent snapshot for this job.

parameters.name: renormalization-window-days
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option. The period over which adjustments to the score are applied, as new data is seen.

parameters.name: results-retention-days
parameters.summary: Advanced configuration option. The period of time (in days) that results are retained. Age is calculated relative to the timestamp of the latest bucket result. If this property has a non-null value, once per day at 00:30 (server time), results that are the specified number of days older than the latest bucket result are deleted from Elasticsearch. The default value is null, which means all results are retained.

parameters.name: groups
parameters.summary: A list of job groups. A job can belong to no groups or many.

parameters.name: detectors
parameters.summary: An array of detector update objects.

parameters.name: per-partition-categorization
parameters.summary: Settings related to how categorization interacts with partition fields.

parameters.name: job-id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the anomaly detection job.

parameters.name: snapshot-id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the model snapshot.

parameters.name: description
parameters.summary: A description of the model snapshot.

parameters.name: retain
parameters.summary: If `true`, this snapshot will not be deleted during automatic cleanup of snapshots older than `model_snapshot_retention_days`. However, this snapshot will be deleted when the job is deleted.

parameters.name: model-id
parameters.summary: The unique identifier of the trained model. Currently, only PyTorch models are supported.

parameters.name: number-of-allocations
parameters.summary: The number of model allocations on each node where the model is deployed. All allocations on a node share the same copy of the model in memory but use a separate set of threads to evaluate the model. Increasing this value generally increases the throughput. If this setting is greater than the number of hardware threads it will automatically be changed to a value less than the number of hardware threads. If adaptive_allocations is enabled, do not set this value, because it’s automatically set.

parameters.name: adaptive-allocations
parameters.summary: Adaptive allocations configuration. When enabled, the number of allocations is set based on the current load. If adaptive_allocations is enabled, do not set the number of allocations manually.

parameters.name: job-id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the anomaly detection job.

parameters.name: snapshot-id
parameters.summary: A numerical character string that uniquely identifies the model snapshot.

parameters.name: wait-for-completion
parameters.summary: When true, the API won’t respond until the upgrade is complete. Otherwise, it responds as soon as the upgrade task is assigned to a node.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.summary: Controls the time to wait for the request to complete.

parameters.name: max-archive-version
parameters.summary: Specifies the maximum `archive_version` to be cleared from the archive.
