parameters.name: debug
parameters.summary: Defines whether the watch runs in debug mode.

parameters.name: action-modes
parameters.summary: Determines how to handle the watch actions as part of the watch execution.

parameters.name: alternative-input
parameters.summary: When present, the watch uses this object as a payload instead of executing its own input.

parameters.name: ignore-condition
parameters.summary: When set to `true`, the watch execution uses the always condition. This can also be specified as an HTTP parameter.

parameters.name: record-execution
parameters.summary: When set to `true`, the watch record representing the watch execution result is persisted to the `.watcher-history` index for the current time. In addition, the status of the watch is updated, possibly throttling subsequent runs. This can also be specified as an HTTP parameter.

parameters.name: trigger-data
parameters.summary: This structure is parsed as the data of the trigger event that will be used during the watch execution.

parameters.name: watch
parameters.summary: When present, this watch is used instead of the one specified in the request. This watch is not persisted to the index and `record_execution` cannot be set.

parameters.name: master-timeout
parameters.summary: The period to wait for a connection to the master node. If no response is received before the timeout expires, the request fails and returns an error.

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: The watch identifier.

parameters.name: id
parameters.summary: The identifier for the watch.

parameters.name: active
parameters.summary: The initial state of the watch. The default value is `true`, which means the watch is active by default.

parameters.name: if-primary-term
parameters.summary: Only update the watch if the last operation that has changed the watch has the specified primary term

parameters.name: if-seq-no
parameters.summary: Only update the watch if the last operation that has changed the watch has the specified sequence number

parameters.name: version
parameters.summary: Explicit version number for concurrency control

parameters.name: actions
parameters.summary: The list of actions that will be run if the condition matches.

parameters.name: condition
parameters.summary: The condition that defines if the actions should be run.

parameters.name: input
parameters.summary: The input that defines the input that loads the data for the watch.

parameters.name: metadata
parameters.summary: Metadata JSON that will be copied into the history entries.

parameters.name: throttle-period
parameters.summary: The minimum time between actions being run. The default is 5 seconds. This default can be changed in the config file with the setting `xpack.watcher.throttle.period.default_period`. If both this value and the `throttle_period_in_millis` parameter are specified, Watcher uses the last parameter included in the request.

parameters.name: throttle-period-in-millis
parameters.summary: Minimum time in milliseconds between actions being run. Defaults to 5000. If both this value and the throttle_period parameter are specified, Watcher uses the last parameter included in the request.

parameters.name: transform
parameters.summary: The transform that processes the watch payload to prepare it for the watch actions.

parameters.name: trigger
parameters.summary: The trigger that defines when the watch should run.

parameters.name: from
parameters.summary: The offset from the first result to fetch. It must be non-negative.

parameters.name: size
parameters.summary: The number of hits to return. It must be non-negative.

parameters.name: query
parameters.summary: A query that filters the watches to be returned.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.summary: One or more fields used to sort the search results.

parameters.name: search-after
parameters.summary: Retrieve the next page of hits using a set of sort values from the previous page.

parameters.name: master-timeout
parameters.summary: Period to wait for a connection to the master node.

parameters.name: metric
parameters.summary: Defines which additional metrics are included in the response.

parameters.name: emit-stacktraces
parameters.summary: Defines whether stack traces are generated for each watch that is running.

parameters.name: master-timeout
parameters.summary: The period to wait for the master node. If the master node is not available before the timeout expires, the request fails and returns an error. To indicate that the request should never timeout, set it to `-1`.

parameters.name: master-timeout

parameters.name: timeout

parameters.name: categories

parameters.name: accept-enterprise

parameters.name: human

parameters.name: notify-when
parameters.summary: Indicates how often alerts generate actions. Valid values include: `onActionGroupChange`: Actions run when the alert status changes; `onActiveAlert`: Actions run when the alert becomes active and at each check interval while the rule conditions are met; `onThrottleInterval`: Actions run when the alert becomes active and at the interval specified in the throttle property while the rule conditions are met. NOTE: You cannot specify `notify_when` at both the rule and action level. The recommended method is to set it for each action. If you set it at the rule level then update the rule in Kibana, it is automatically changed to use action-specific values.

parameters.name: objects
parameters.summary: A list of objects to export. NOTE: this optional parameter cannot be combined with the `types` option

parameters.name: search
parameters.summary: Search for documents to export using the Elasticsearch Simple Query String syntax.

parameters.name: type
parameters.summary: The saved object types to include in the export. Use `*` to export all the types. Valid options depend on enabled plugins, but may include `visualization`, `dashboard`, `search`, `index-pattern`, `tag`, `config`, `config-global`, `lens`, `map`, `event-annotation-group`, `query`, `url`, `action`, `alert`, `alerting_rule_template`, `apm-indices`, `cases-user-actions`, `cases`, `cases-comments`, `infrastructure-monitoring-log-view`, `ml-trained-model`, `osquery-saved-query`, `osquery-pack`, `osquery-pack-asset`.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.summary: Overwrites saved objects when they already exist. When used, potential conflict errors are automatically resolved by overwriting the destination object. NOTE: This option cannot be used with the `createNewCopies` option.

parameters.name: create-new-copies
parameters.summary: Creates copies of saved objects, regenerates each object ID, and resets the origin. When used, potential conflict errors are avoided. NOTE: This option cannot be used with the `overwrite` and `compatibilityMode` options.

parameters.name: compatibility-mode
parameters.summary: Applies various adjustments to the saved objects that are being imported to maintain compatibility between different Kibana versions. Use this option only if you encounter issues with imported saved objects. NOTE: This option cannot be used with the `createNewCopies` option.

parameters.name: file
parameters.summary: A file exported using the export API. Changing the contents of the exported file in any way before importing it can cause errors, crashes or data loss. NOTE: The `savedObjects.maxImportExportSize` configuration setting limits the number of saved objects which may be included in this file. Similarly, the `savedObjects.maxImportPayloadBytes` setting limits the overall size of the file that can be imported.

parameters.name: create-new-copies
parameters.summary: Creates copies of saved objects, regenerates each object ID, and resets the origin.

parameters.name: compatibility-mode
parameters.summary: Applies adjustments to maintain compatibility between different Kibana versions.

commands.name: attack-discovery-find

parameters.name: alert-ids
parameters.summary: Filter results to Attack discoveries that include any of the provided alert IDs

parameters.name: connector-names
parameters.summary: Filter results to Attack discoveries created by any of the provided human readable connector names. Note that values must match the human readable `connector_name` property of an Attack discovery, e.g. "GPT-5 Chat", which are distinct from `connector_id` values used to generate Attack discoveries.

parameters.name: enable-field-rendering
parameters.summary: Enables a markdown syntax used to render pivot fields, for example `{{ user.name james }}`. When disabled, the same example would be rendered as `james`. This is primarily used for Attack discovery views within Kibana. Defaults to `false`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.summary: End of the time range for the search. Accepts absolute timestamps (ISO 8601) or relative date math (e.g. "now", "now-24h").

parameters.name: ids
parameters.summary: Filter results to the Attack discoveries with the specified IDs

parameters.name: include-unique-alert-ids
parameters.summary: If `true`, the response will include `unique_alert_ids` and `unique_alert_ids_count` aggregated across the matched Attack discoveries

parameters.name: page
parameters.summary: Page number to return (used for pagination). Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: per-page
parameters.summary: Number of Attack discoveries to return per page (used for pagination). Defaults to 10.

parameters.name: search
parameters.summary: Free-text search query applied to relevant text fields of Attack discoveries (title, description, tags, etc.)

parameters.name: shared
parameters.summary: Whether to filter by shared visibility. If omitted, both shared and privately visible Attack discoveries are returned. Use `true` to return only shared discoveries, `false` to return only those visible to the current user.

parameters.name: scheduled
parameters.summary: Whether to filter by scheduled or ad-hoc attack discoveries. If omitted, both types of attack discoveries are returned. Use `true` to return only scheduled discoveries or `false` to return only ad-hoc discoveries.

parameters.name: sort-field
parameters.summary: Field used to sort results. See `AttackDiscoveryFindSortField` for allowed values.

parameters.name: sort-order
parameters.summary: Sort order direction `asc` for ascending or `desc` for descending. Defaults to `desc`.

parameters.name: start
parameters.summary: Start of the time range for the search. Accepts absolute timestamps (ISO 8601) or relative date math (e.g. "now-7d").

parameters.name: status
parameters.summary: Filter by alert workflow status. Provide one or more of the allowed workflow states.

parameters.name: with-replacements
parameters.summary: When true, return the created Attack discoveries with text replacements applied to the detailsMarkdown, entitySummaryMarkdown, summaryMarkdown, and title fields. Defaults to `true`.

parameters.name: request-id
parameters.summary: An optional idempotency token - if two create requests share the same request_id token (min size 32 characters, max 128) then only one deployment will be created, the second request will return the info of that deployment (in the same format described below, but with blanks for auth-related fields)

parameters.name: validate-only
parameters.summary: If true, will just validate the Deployment definition but will not perform the creation

parameters.name: template-id
parameters.summary: An optional template id - if present, the referenced template will be used to fill in the resources field of the deployment creation request. If any resources are present in the request together with the template, the ones coming in the request will prevail and no merging with the template will be performed.

parameters.name: minimal-metadata
parameters.summary: Comma separated list of attributes to include in response for deployments found. Useful for reducing response size when retrieving many deployments. Use of this parameter moves the result to the minimal_metadata section of the response.

parameters.name: cloud-version
parameters.summary: The version of the Elasticsearch cluster cluster that will potentially be configured to have remote clusters.

parameters.name: deployment-id
parameters.summary: Identifier for the Deployment

parameters.name: show-security
parameters.summary: Whether to include the Elasticsearch 2.x security information in the response - can be large per cluster and also include credentials

parameters.name: show-metadata
parameters.summary: Whether to include the full cluster metadata in the response - can be large per cluster and also include credentials

parameters.name: show-plans
parameters.summary: Whether to include the full current and pending plan information in the response - can be large per cluster

parameters.name: show-plan-logs
parameters.summary: Whether to include with the current and pending plan information the attempt log - can be very large per cluster

parameters.name: show-plan-history
parameters.summary:
Whether to include the plan history with the current and pending plan information. The results can be very large per cluster.
 By default, if a given resource kind (e.g. Elasticsearch, Kibana, etc.) has more than 100 plans
 (which should be very rare, most likely caused by a bug) only 100 plans are returned for the given resource type:
 The first 10 plans, and the last 90 plans for that resource type.
 If ALL of the plans are desired, pass the `force_all_plan_history` parameter with a value of `true`.

parameters.name: show-plan-defaults
parameters.summary: If showing plans, whether to show values that are left at their default value (less readable but more informative)

parameters.name: convert-legacy-plans
parameters.summary: If showing plans, whether to leave pre-2.0.0 plans in their legacy format (the default), or whether to update them to 2.0.x+ format (if 'true')

parameters.name: show-system-alerts
parameters.summary: Number of system alerts (such as forced restarts due to memory limits) to be included in the response - can be large per cluster. Negative numbers or 0 will not return field.

parameters.name: show-settings
parameters.summary: Whether to show cluster settings in the response.

parameters.name: show-instance-metrics
parameters.summary: Whether to show resources instance metrics in the response.

parameters.name: show-instance-configurations
parameters.summary: If true, will return details for each instance configuration referenced by the deployment.

parameters.name: enrich-with-template
parameters.summary: If showing plans, whether to enrich the plan by including the missing elements from the deployment template it is based on

parameters.name: force-all-plan-history
parameters.summary:
Force show the entire plan history no matter how long.
 As noted in the `show_plan_history` parameter description, by default, a maximum of 100 plans are shown per resource. 
 If `true`, this parameter overrides the default, and ALL plans are returned.
 Use with care as the plan history can be VERY large. Consider pairing with `show_plan_logs=false`.

parameters.summary: Whether to include the full cluster metadata in the response - can be large per cluster and also include credentials.

parameters.name: show-plans
parameters.summary: Whether to include the full current and pending plan information in the response - can be large per cluster.

parameters.name: show-plan-logs
parameters.summary: Whether to include with the current and pending plan information the attempt log - can be very large per cluster.

parameters.name: show-plan-history
parameters.summary: Whether to include with the current and pending plan information the plan history- can be very large per cluster.

parameters.name: show-plan-defaults
parameters.summary: If showing plans, whether to show values that are left at their default value (less readable but more informative).

parameters.name: convert-legacy-plans
parameters.summary: If showing plans, whether to leave pre-2.0.0 plans in their legacy format (the default), or whether to update them to 2.0.x+ format (if 'true').

parameters.name: show-settings
parameters.summary: Whether to show cluster settings in the response.

parameters.name: clear-transient
parameters.summary: If set (defaults to false) then removes the transient section from all child resources, making it safe to reapply via an update
